theme.theme-annotation: Blue-dominant study theme ‚Äî deep royal blue palette with subdued gold accent. Blue is the dominant colour throughout surfaces, borders, text, and interactive elements. Gold appears only as a quiet highlight. Authoritative and clear.

elements.text1: Akwai ra‚Äôayoyi daban-daban akan Allah da kuma yaya Allah yake. Samun ganewar yaya Allah yake a zahirance. To dole kuma mu duba me Littafi Mai Tsarki ya fa…óa yarda Allah ya bayyana kansa a gare mu acikin Littafinsa Mai Tsarki
elements.text2: Hol no Alla wayi tigi rigi? Yim…ìe ina njogii miijooji kala ‚Äî won heen ina cikka ko o wo…ó…óu…óo, mo anndaaka, won heen ko doole mo∆¥∆¥e, walla laam…óo kosmos. Kono en ngalaa haaju e teskaade. Alla hollitii en hoore mum e nder deftere nde, ko …óoon pu…ó…óorto…óen.
elements.text3: What is God really like? People have all sorts of ideas ‚Äî some think of Him as distant and unknowable, others as a kind force, or a cosmic rule-giver. But we don‚Äôt have to guess. God has revealed Himself to us in the Bible, and that is where we begin.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan ayar ta bayyana Allah a matsayin ‚ÄôUban Ubangijinmu Yesu Almasihu‚Äô, wanda hakan ke nuna cewa Allah ba wata ∆ôarfin da ba shi da rai ba ne, sai dai shi mutum ne mai rai wanda yake da dangantakar Uba da ∆äa tare da Yesu. Hakan kuma yana nuna cewa Allah wani ne da za mu iya gode masa da kuma magana da shi ta wurin addu‚Äôa.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya yi amfani da wata take ta musamman ga Allah a nan ‚Äî me wannan take ke gaya maka game da irin dangantakar da Allah yake da ita tare da Yesu, da kuma me hakan ke …óauke game da yanayin sa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Oo aaye hollitii Allah ko ‚ÄôNyaako Joomiraawo mee…óen Yeesu Almasiihu‚Äô, ko hollita Alla wanaa doole mo walaa yonki, ammaa o woni ne…ó…óo nguurndam mo jogii jokkondiral Baaba e ∆Åi…ó…óo e Yeesu. ∆äum kadi hollita Alla woni mo mba…óaten njettoor e haaldirde e makko e nder do‚Äôaare.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus huutorii innde he…ì…ìundi sanne ngam Alla …óoo ‚Äî hol ko ndee innde anndinta ma e ko woni jokkondiral Alla e Yeesu, e hol ko …óum hollita dow yontere muu…óum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: This verse identifies God as ‚Äôthe Father of our Lord Jesus Christ‚Äô, revealing that God is not an impersonal force but a personal being who stands in a Father-Son relationship with Jesus. It also shows that God is someone to whom we can give thanks and speak to in prayer.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses a very specific title for God here ‚Äî what does that title tell you about the kind of relationship God has with Jesus, and what might that imply about His nature?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan ayar tana nuna cewa halitta kanta shaida ce ta kasancewar Allah da …óaukakarsa. Sararin sama ba ya tsaya tsaka-tsaki ‚Äî yana ‚Äôbayyana‚Äô wani abu game da Allah. Kamar yadda wata zane-zane ke nuna mai zanen ta, haka kuma duniyar halitta ke nuna Mahalicci. Shaida ta Allah ba a …ìoye ta ba ‚Äî tana iya ganuwa a sama a kowace rana.
elements.questionHint1: Idan aka ce sama yana ‚Äôbayyana‚Äô wani abu, me hakan ke nuna game da abin da mai lura da duniyar halitta ya kamata ya iya yanke hukunci a kai?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ndee aaye hollitii tagooje he…ì…ìude hoore mum ina seeda wonde Alla ina woodi e teddungal muu…óum. Ngurleeji …ói ngalaa miijo ‚Äî ina njannginiri feere e ko fayti e Alla. Ko nii …óerewol hollita mo windii, ko non kadi aduna tagooje hollita Tag…óo. Seede Alla walaa nder suudu woo ‚Äî ina yiya e dow men koo …óume.
elements.questionHint2: To ngurleeji ngoni njannginta feere, hol ko …óum hollita e ko yim…ìe ndaari aduna tagooje mbaNnginata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The verse suggests that creation itself is a witness to God‚Äôs existence and glory. The sky and heavens are not neutral ‚Äî they actively ‚Äôdeclare‚Äô and ‚Äôdisplay‚Äô something about God. Just as a piece of artwork points to an artist, the created world points to a Creator. Evidence for God is not hidden away; it is visible above us every day.
elements.questionHint3: If the heavens are said to ‚Äôdeclare‚Äô something, what does that imply about what an observer of the natural world should be able to conclude?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Misalin ‚Äôsa∆ôa‚Äô yana nuna cewa rayuwar …óan adam ba ha…óari ba ce ko wani lamari na halitta kawai, sai dai aiki ne na gangan na Allah tare da kula. Kamar yadda masa∆ôi ke aiki da zaren …óaya bayan …óaya da niyyar gani da kula, haka Allah ya shiga cikin ∆ôir∆ôirar kowane mutum da kansa. Hakan yana nuna cewa Allah yana da cikakken sani da kuma kula da kowane mutum daga farkon rayuwarsa.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da abin da sa∆ôa ke …óauke da shi ‚Äî za ka/ki ce aiki ne na sakaci da gudu-gudu, ko kuwa na gangan da kula? Me hakan ke gaya maka game da yadda Allah ya tsara ka/ki?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Siftorde ‚Äôwo…ó…óude‚Äô hollita nguurndam aadee wanaa ha…óeende walla ko wa…ó…óaani, ammaa ko ba…óngal gollal Alla ceerngal e miijo. Ko nii foofoowo golli foofo e foofo e miijo, ko non Allah wa…ói gollal muu…óum e tagude koo ne…ó…óo. ∆äum hollita Alla jogii ande ceerngal e nunaare ne…ó…óo koo gooto daga fu…ó…óorde nguurndam muu…óum.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo ko wo…ó…óude haani ‚Äî mbaa…óaa wiyde ko gollal ladde mo faayre, koo ko gollal miijo e nunaare? Hol ko …óum anndinta ma e no Alla tagii ma?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The image of being ‚Äôwoven together‚Äô suggests that human life is not an accident or a random biological event, but an intentional, careful act of craftsmanship by God. Just as a weaver works deliberately, thread by thread, God was personally involved in forming each person. This points to God‚Äôs intimate knowledge of and care for every individual human being from the very beginning of their existence.
elements.questionHint3: Think about what weaving involves ‚Äî would you describe it as a careless, random process, or a deliberate and careful one? What does that tell you about how God formed you?

elements.passageText1: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi wa…óansu mu‚Äôujizai da yawa dabam dabam a gaban almajiran, wa…óanda ba a rubuta a littafin nan ba. <sup>31</sup>Amma an rubuta wa…óannan ne, domin ku ba da gaskiya Yesu shi ne Almasihu, ∆äan Allah, ta wurin gaskatawa kuma ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText2: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi alamun ban mamaki da yawa a idanun almajiransa wa…óanda ba a rubuta a wannan littafi ba. <sup>31</sup>Amma an rubuta wa…óannan ne, domin ku ba da gaskiya cewa Yesu shi ne Almasihu, ∆äan Allah. SaÀàan nan kuma, ta wurin ba da gaskiya ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi wa…óansu abubuwan banmamaki da yawa a gaban almajiransa da ba a rubuta a wannan littafi ba. <sup>31</sup>An dai rubuta wa…óannan ne don ku gaskata Yesu ne Kiristi, ∆äan Allah kuma ta wurin gaskatawa ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>30</sup>Yeesu wa…óii ko hay…óinii feere‚Äëfeere …óu…ó…óum yeeso tokku…ìemo …óum windaaka nder …óerewol ngo‚Äôol. <sup>31</sup>Ammaa …óu‚Äôum …óum windaa ngam kooloo…óon Yeesu woni Almasiihu ∆Åi…ó…óo Allah, nden laawol hoolaakimo ke…ìon yonki ki re‚Äôataa barka inde maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>30</sup>Now Jesus performed many other miraculous signs in the presence of the disciples, which are not recorded in this book. <sup>31</sup>But these are recorded so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.

elements.question1: Me ya sa Yohanna ya ce ya rubuta wa…óannan alamu? Me wannan ya gaya mana game da manufar Littafi Mai Tsarki?
elements.question2: Hol ko wa…ói Yuhanna wiyde o winndii …óee maandeeji? Hol ko …óum anndini en e faandaare Biibal ngal?
elements.question3: Why does John say he recorded these signs? What does this tell us about the purpose of the Bible?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya ce ya rubuta wa…óannan alamu domin masu karatu su ba da gaskiya cewa Yesu shi ne Almasihu, ∆äan Allah, kuma ta wurin gaskatawa su sami rai a cikin sunansa. Hakan yana gaya mana cewa Littafi Mai Tsarki ba tarihi kawai ba ne ko tarin koyarwar …óabi‚Äôa ‚Äî yana da manufa ta musamman ta kawo mutane zuwa bangaskiya da kuma, ta wurin bangaskiya, zuwa rai.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya fara gaya mana kai tsaye abin da yake fatan ganin a masu karantawa ‚Äî za ka iya ganin burin biyu da ya ambata, kuma ta yaya suke da ala∆ôa da juna?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yuhanna wi‚Äôi o winndii …óee maandeeji ngam jooloo…ìe …ìen njaa…ìa koolugol Yeesu woni Almasiihu ∆Åi…ó…óo Allah, nden laawol hoolaakimo ke…ìon yonki ki re‚Äôataa barka innde maako. ∆äum anndina en Biibal ngal wanaa tan …óerewol laamu walla ke…ìngo naataneeji laawol ‚Äî o jogii faandaare njannginde yim…ìe nguurndam tan. Windaama ngam wa…óde yim…ìe njaa…ìa hoolagol, nde laawol nden, yonki.
elements.questionHint2: Yuhanna hollita tii…óu…óo ko faandaare maako e jooloo…ìe …ìen ‚Äî mbaa…óaa yi‚Äôude faandaare …ói…ói mo holliti, e no …ói njokki e rewde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John says he recorded these signs so that readers would come to believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and through that belief find life in his name. This tells us that the Bible is not merely a historical record or a collection of moral teachings ‚Äî it has an intentional, evangelistic purpose. It is written to bring people to faith and, through faith, to life.
elements.questionHint3: John tells us directly what outcome he was hoping for in his readers ‚Äî can you spot the two goals he mentions, and how are they connected to each other?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>A zamanin dƒÅ, Allah ya yi wa kakannin kakanninmu magana ta hanyoyi masu yawa iri iri, ta bakin annabawa, <sup>2</sup>amma a zamanin nan na ∆ôarshe, sai ya yi mana magana ta wurin ∆äansa, wanda ya sa magƒÅjin kome, wanda ta wurinsa ne kuma ya halicci duniya. <sup>3</sup>Shi ne hasken …óaukakar Allah, ainihin kamannin zatinsa kuma, shi kuma yana a ri∆ôe da dukan abubuwa ta ikon fa…óarsa. Bayan da ya tsarkake zunubanmu, sai ya zauna a dama da Ma…óaukaki a can Sama.
elements.passageText2: <sup>1</sup>A zamanin dƒÅ, Allah ya yi wa kakanninmu magana ta hanyoyi iri-iri da kuma a lokatai dabam-dabam ta wurin annabawa. <sup>2</sup>Amma a wa…óannan kwanakin ∆ôarshe ya yi mana magana ta wurin ∆äansa. Ta wurin ∆äan ne Allah ya halicci kome da kome. Shi ne kuma ya zama magƒÅjin kome. <sup>3</sup>Shi ne hasken …óaukakar Allah da kuma ainihin kamannin Allah. Daidai yake kuma da Allah. Yana ri∆ôe da dukan kome ta wurin kalmarsa mai iko. Bayan ya tsabtace mu daga zunubanmu, sai ya zauna a sama a hannun daman Mai ∆äaukaka duka.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>A dƒÅ Allah ya yi wa kakanni-kakanninmu magana ta bakin annabawa a lokuta dabam-dabam da kuma a hanyoyi iri-iri, <sup>2</sup>amma a wa…óannan kwanaki a ∆ôarshe ya yi magana da mu ta wurin ∆äansa, wanda ya na…óa magƒÅjin dukan abubuwa, wanda kuma ta wurinsa ya halicci duniya. <sup>3</sup>∆äan shi ne hasken …óaukakar Allah, shi ne kuma ainihin kamanninsa, yana ri∆ôe da dukan abubuwa ta wurin kalmarsa mai iko. Bayan ya yi tsarkakewa don zunubai, sai ya zauna a hannun daman Ma…óaukaki a sama.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>Ndenno Allah metanii maama‚Äôen mee…óen nde …óu…ó…óum e …óate …óu…ó…óe daga kondu…óe annabo‚Äôen. <sup>2</sup>Ammaa nder bal…óe …óe‚Äôe …óe re‚Äôuki duuniyaaru o metiranii‚Äôen ∆Åi…ó…óo maako, mo o su…ìi warta donoowo koo …óume. ∆äonmaa barka maako Allah tagi duuniyaaru. <sup>3</sup>E mo holla jayngol teddungal Allah, nden nder koo …óume e mo nandi e Allah. E mo jogorii koo …óume wolde maako goodunde baw…óe. Ga…óa o wa…óii laawol yaafaaki hakke, o joo…óii ton dow to jungo nyaamo ngo Allah Joomu Mangu.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>After God spoke long ago in various portions and in various ways to our ancestors through the prophets, <sup>2</sup>in these last days he has spoken to us in a son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom he created the world. <sup>3</sup>The Son is the radiance of his glory and the representation of his essence, and he sustains all things by his powerful word, and so when he had accomplished cleansing for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Allah ya yi mana magana, bisa ga wannan nassin? Me yake gaya mana game da dangantakar Yesu da Allah?
elements.question2: Hol no Alla haaldirta e men, fawaade e ndee …óoo tonngoode? Hol ko nde haalanta en e jokkondiral Iisaa e Alla?
elements.question3: How has God spoken to us, according to this passage? What does it tell us about Jesus‚Äô relationship to God?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bisa ga wannan nassi, Allah ya yi magana a zamanin da ta bakin annabawa ta hanyoyi da dama da kuma lokuta daban-daban, amma yanzu ya yi mana magana a ∆ôarshe ta wurin ∆äansa Yesu. Wannan nassi yana yin da‚Äôawa masu girma game da dangantakar Yesu da Allah: shi ne ‚Äôhasken …óaukakar Allah‚Äô da kuma ‚Äôainihin kamannin zatinsa‚Äô, ma‚Äôana ganin Yesu shi ne ganin Allah a hakika. Yesu kuma ya halicci duniya, yana ri∆ôe da dukan abubuwa, kuma ya tsarkake zunubai ‚Äî duk wa…óannan suna nuna cewa shi yana da ainihin asalin Allah.
elements.questionHint1: Wannan nassi yana bambanta yadda Allah ya yi magana ‚Äôa zamanin da‚Äô da yadda ya yi magana ‚Äôa zamanin nan na ∆ôarshe‚Äô ‚Äî me ya sha bamban tsakanin hanyoyin biyu, kuma me bayani game da ∆äan a ayar 3 ke gaya maka game da yadda yake da ala∆ôa da Allah kansa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Fawaade e ndee tonngoode, Alla haaldiri e …ìesngu mee…óen daga ada e nder …óate …óu…ó…óe e laabi …óu…ó…ói daga kondu…óe annabo‚Äôen, ammaa jooni o haaldiranii‚Äôen haa timmi e nder ∆Åi…ó…óo maako Yeesu. Ndee tonngoode wa…óii miijooji maw…ói dow jokkondiral Yeesu e Alla: o woni ‚Äôjayngol teddungal Allah‚Äô e ‚Äônandi e Allah haa timmi‚Äô, ko hollita yi‚Äôugol Yeesu ko yi‚Äôugol Alla goonga. Yeesu kadi tagi duuniyaaru, jogorii koo …óume, o wa…óii yaafaaki hakke ‚Äî fow …ói hollita o jogii yontere ceerngal Allah.
elements.questionHint2: Ndee tonngoode hollita laawol Alla haaldiri ‚Äôndenno‚Äô e laawol o haaldiri ‚Äôjooni nder bal…óe …óe re‚Äôuki‚Äô ‚Äî hol ko waylii hakkunde laabi …ói…ói …ói, e hol ko siftorde ∆Åi…ó…óo on nder aaya 3 anndinta ma e ko ∆Åi…ó…óo on jokki …ìuri heen e Alla hoore muu…óum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: According to this passage, God spoke in the past through the prophets in many different ways, but has now spoken definitively and finally through His Son, Jesus. The passage makes extraordinary claims about Jesus‚Äô relationship to God: he is the ‚Äôradiance of his glory‚Äô and the ‚Äôrepresentation of his essence‚Äô, meaning that to see Jesus is to see what God is truly like. Jesus also created the world, sustains all things, and has now accomplished the cleansing of sins ‚Äî pointing to a unique divine identity.
elements.questionHint3: The passage contrasts how God spoke ‚Äôlong ago‚Äô with how he has spoken ‚Äôin these last days‚Äô ‚Äî what is different about the two ways, and what does the description of the Son in verse 3 tell you about how closely he is linked to God himself?

elements.sampleAnswer3: For God to be ‚Äôholy‚Äô means He is utterly set apart ‚Äî morally perfect, pure, and completely unlike anything in the created world. The threefold repetition (‚Äòholy, holy, holy‚Äô) is the strongest form of emphasis in Hebrew ‚Äî it is not just saying God is very holy, but that holiness defines His very being to the highest possible degree. elements.questionHint3: In everyday language, if you want to stress how extremely something is true, what do you do ‚Äî and how might that same idea apply to why the angels repeat the word ‚Äôholy‚Äô three times rather than just once?

elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus makes a remarkable claim: that to have seen Him is to have seen the Father. elements.sampleAnswer3: This passage tells us that God exists outside of and before time itself ‚Äî He was God before the earth or mountains existed, and He will continue to be God forever. Verse 4 shows that God does not experience time as we do: what feels like a thousand years to us is like a single day to Him. elements.sampleAnswer3: It means that God‚Äôs character, His love, His promises, and His goodness never shift or fluctuate. elements.sampleAnswer3: This passage tells us that God is omnipresent ‚Äî there is literally nowhere in the universe, from the heights of heaven to the depths of Sheol, where God is absent. Ammaa wiyde Allah woni yidde hollita yidde nde wowlaani e wonaare muu…óum; o waawataa heccugol yiddude fay ko nii o wawataaki heccugol wonde Alla. ∆äum hollita yidde muu…óum wanaa jaabawol e men ngon…óen ka…ìatoo…ìe yiddugol ‚Äî ina ∆¥iwa daga ko o woni. ∆äum kadi hollita yidde wanaa tan miijo walla mii…óo Alla jogii, ammaa …ìernde yontere muu…óum, ko hokka en gorol hoolagol yidde muu…óum woni newnii…óo, waylitataako, e timmaani.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e sa…ìaare hakkunde wi‚Äôugol ‚Äôjanngu…óo o mo∆¥∆¥i‚Äô e ‚Äôjanngu…óo o mo∆¥∆¥inay koo addan‚Äô ‚Äî hol ko …ìuri yiyde e yontere muu…óum timmunde, e no mbaydi nden nawdata e ko Yaya wi‚Äôi e Alla?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Saying God loves is saying something He does ‚Äî an action He can choose or withhold. But saying God is love means love is inseparable from His very being; He cannot stop being loving any more than He can stop being God. It means His love is not a response to us being lovable ‚Äî it flows from who He is. This also means that love is not just a feeling God has but the core of His identity, which gives us grounds to trust that His love is permanent, unconditional, and inexhaustible.
elements.questionHint3: Consider the difference between saying ‚Äôa teacher is kind‚Äô and ‚Äôa teacher sometimes acts kindly‚Äô ‚Äî which says more about their core identity, and how does that distinction apply to what John is claiming about God?

elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>But God demonstrates his own love for us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce Allah ya nuna ∆ôaunarsa ta wurin mutuwar Almasihu a madadin mu. Abin da ke jan hankali ∆ôwarai shi ne lokacin: Allah bai jira mu gyara rayuwarmu ba, mu sami falalarsa, ko mu zama cancantaccen ∆ôaunarsa. Almasihu ya mutu dominmu tun muna masu zunubi ‚Äî har yanzu muna tawaye ga Allah. Hakan yana juya zato na …óan adam cewa soyayya dole ta cancanta. ∆òaunar Allah ba lada ba ce ga kyakkyawan …óabi‚Äôa; ita ce kyauta ce ta farko da ta ∆¥anci, wadda aka baiwa wa…óanda ba su yi komai ba don su cancanta.
elements.questionHint1: A mafi yawan ala∆ôar mutum, muna nuna kirki ga wa…óanda suka nuna kirki a gare mu ‚Äî don haka me ke ban mamaki ko wahala fahimta game da lokacin da Bulus ya ce Allah ya za…ìa bayyana ∆ôaunarsa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi‚Äôi Allah hollii yidde muu…óum e nder maayugol Almasiihu ngam mee…óen. Ko kayni haa timmii ko wakkati ngoo: Alla jaa…ìaay en mba…óaten dilli nguurndam mee…óen, ke…ìen baarka muu…óum, koo mba…óaten ndaNngataa yiddugol muu…óum. Almasiihu maayii ngam mee…óen tawi ngon…óen nder hakke ‚Äî ngon…óen tawi e fiyakugol Alla. ∆äum waylitita taa…óiinde aadirini yidde dina haani wa…óanee…óo. Yidde Alla wanaa jaafal kaa gollal mo∆¥∆¥e; o woni yidde newnii…óo, o fu…ó…óii e kanko koo, nde hokki …ìeen ngo‚Äôi ko nga…óii …óume fuu ngam ke…ìugol.
elements.questionHint2: E jokkondirali aadee fow, mba…óaten hollata mo∆¥∆¥ere …ìeen hollitii en mo∆¥∆¥ere ‚Äî ko …óume wayi kaa sappungol koo yo‚Äôori e wakkati he…ì…ìu…óo Bulus wi‚Äôi Alla su…ìii hollugol yidde muu…óum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says God demonstrated His love through the death of Christ on our behalf. What is especially striking is the timing: God did not wait for us to clean up our lives, earn His favour, or become worthy of His love. Christ died for us while we were still sinners ‚Äî still in rebellion against God. This overturns the human assumption that love must be deserved. God‚Äôs love is not a reward for good behaviour; it is a free, initiating love given to those who had done nothing to merit it.
elements.questionHint3: In most human relationships, we tend to show kindness to people who have been kind to us ‚Äî so what is surprising or unexpected about the specific moment Paul says God chose to demonstrate His love?

elements.passageText4: <sup>9</sup>Allah hollii‚Äôen yidde muu…óum nde o liloyi ∆Åi…ó…óo maako go‚Äôoto tan mo o woodi nder duuniyaaru, ngam ke…ìen yonki ki re‚Äôataa barka ∆Åi…ó…óo on. <sup>10</sup>Raa no yidde nde goonga wa‚Äôi, naa‚Äô enen ngi…ói Allah, ammaa Allah yi…ói‚Äôen o liloyi ∆Åi…ó…óo maako ngam warta cakkiri yaafaaki hakke mee…óen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>9</sup>By this the love of God is revealed in us: that God has sent his one and only Son into the world so that we may live through him. <sup>10</sup>In this is love: not that we have loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna yana bayyana a sarari cewa Allah ne ya …óauki matakin farko ‚Äî ∆ôauna an bayyana ta ne ba a matsayin ∆ôaunarmu ga Allah ba, sai dai ∆ôaunarsa a gare mu. Allah ya zo da farko. Ya aika da ∆äansa kafin mu neme shi, domin yin hul…óa da zunubin da ya raba mu da shi. Ba mu jawo ∆ôaunar Allah a gare mu ta wurin imani da himmarmu ba; ya ∆ôaunace mu kyauta kuma ya aiki akan wannan ∆ôauna tare da babbar farashin da ya biya, ta aike da ∆äansa maka…óaici hadaya.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce ‚Äôba mu ne muka ∆ôaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ∆ôaunace mu‚Äô ‚Äî wane ne ya ce ya yi matakin farko, kuma me bambancin da hakan zai yi a gare ka/ki da kanka/kanki sanin cewa dangantakar ta fara ne da Allah, ba da kai/ke ba?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya hollita tii…óu…óo Alla fu…ó…óii ‚Äî yidde nde hollitaama …óoo wanaa yidde mee…óen e Alla, ammaa yidde muu…óum e men. Alla fu…ó…óii. O lilii ∆Åi…ó…óo maako …ìe mba…óen nda…ó…óii e makko, ngam wa…óde laawol hakke ngo waylitiri men e makko. ∆äum anndina en yidde Alla wanaa jaabawol koo waylitirtee…óo ‚Äî …óum woni yidde fu…ó…ói…óo e ∆¥ettaaki sariya. En ngaNnditaay yidde Alla e men e nder hoolagol mee…óen; o yi…ói‚Äôen newnii…óo o gollorii e ndee yidde e nder nafa mawnii, lilugol ∆Åi…ó…óo maako ceerngal ko cakkiri.
elements.questionHint2: Yaya wi‚Äôi ‚Äônaa‚Äô enen ngi…ói Allah, ammaa o yi…ói‚Äôen‚Äô ‚Äî hol ko o wi‚Äôi fu…ó…óii, e no waylitu…óo ko wa…óata e ma hoore ma sande andugol jokkondiral ngol fu…ó…óii e Alla, hoto e maa?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John is explicit that God took the initiative ‚Äî love is defined here not as our love for God, but as His love for us. God moved first. He sent His Son before we sought Him, to deal with the sin that separated us from Him. This tells us that God‚Äôs love is not reactive or conditional ‚Äî it is originating and unconditional. We do not draw God‚Äôs love out of Him by our devotion; He loved us freely and acted on that love at great cost, sending His only Son as a sacrifice.
elements.questionHint3: John says ‚Äônot that we have loved God, but that he loved us‚Äô ‚Äî who does he say made the first move, and what practical difference might it make to you personally to know that the relationship started with God, not with you?

elements.text1: Akwai banbanci ga yarda wasu suka cewar, Wai Yesu ba tun fil‚Äôazal yake ba amma ya kasance ne kawai lokacin da Maryamu ta haife shi a Baitalami. Yesu yana nan tun kafin farkon komai.
elements.text2: Heew…ìe …óon miijoo Yeesu kanko woni moodibbo maw…óo malla ne…ó…óo ko∆¥o mo wuuri duu…ìi ujunnaaje …ói…ói nane. Ammaa Linjiila …óon holla en ne…ó…óo …ìuri …óum mawnugo. elements.text3: Many people think of Jesus as a great teacher or a good man who lived two thousand years ago. But the Bible presents Someone far greater. Surprisingly, Jesus did not begin at Bethlehem ‚Äî He has always existed. In this study we look at who Jesus really is.

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Tun fil‚Äôazal akwai Kalma, Kalman nan kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman nan kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne tun fil‚Äôazal yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Dukan abubuwa sun kasance ta gare shi ne, ba kuma abin da ya kasance na abubuwan da suka kasance, sai ta game da shi.
elements.passageText2: <sup>1</sup>Tun farko akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne wanda tun farko yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Ta wurinsa ne aka yi dukan abubuwa, kuma babu wani abu a cikin dukan halittar da aka yi wanda ba tare da shi aka yi ba.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>Tun farar farawa akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa yana nan tare da Allah, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Yana nan tare da Allah tun farar farawa. <sup>3</sup>Ta wurin Kalman ne aka halicci dukan abubuwa. In ba tare da shi ba, babu abin da aka yi wanda aka yi.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi‚Äôetee…óo Wolde e …óon, e mo wondi e Allah, nden o Allah. <sup>2</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi‚Äôetee…óo Wolde on e wondi e Allah. <sup>3</sup>O tagii koo …óume. Walaa go…ó…óum nder fuu ko tagaa …óum um tagiraayi laawol maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. <sup>2</sup>The Word was with God in the beginning. <sup>3</sup>All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.
elements.note: In this passage the ‚ÄôWord‚Äô is referring to Jesus.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Kafin zuwan sa duniya, Yesu ‚Äî Kalma ‚Äî ya riga ya wanzu. Yana tare da Allah tun fil‚Äôazal, ma‚Äôana koyaushe yana cikin kusancin dangantaka da Uba. Fiye da haka, shi kansa Allah ne gaba …óaya. Yohanna kuma yana gaya mana cewa dukan abubuwa an halicci su ta wurinsa, ma‚Äôana Yesu ba halitta ba ne da ya bayyana a wani lokaci a tarihi, sai dai Mahalicci na har abada wanda ya kasance kafin komai ya wanzu.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce Kalma yana nan ‚Äôtun fil‚Äôazal‚Äô ‚Äî shin wannan yana nuna cewa Yesu yana da farko, ko yana nuna cewa ya riga ya kasance kafin komai ya fara? Me kuma Yohanna ya ce game da ransa kafin halitta?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ko o wara duniyaaru, Yeesu ‚Äî Wolde on ‚Äî e …óon woodi gila. O wondi e Allah daga ko duuniyaaru tagee, ko hollita o wonnoo gila e nder jokkondiral …ìuri …ìuu…óde e Baaba on. ∆Åuri …óum, o woni Allah kanko tigi haa timmi. Yaya kadi hollita koo …óume tagaa e laawol maako, ko hollita Yeesu wanaa ko tagaa o yiitii e saha go…ó…óo nder tariik, ammaa o woni Tag…óo mo wonnoo daga ko ada ko tagaa woni fuu.
elements.questionHint2: Yaya wi‚Äôi Wolde on wonnoo ‚Äôdaga ko duuniyaaru tagee‚Äô ‚Äî …óum hollita Yeesu fu…ó…óiti, koo o …óon woodi …ìe ada ko koo …óume fu…ó…óiti? ∆äume kadi Yaya wi‚Äôi dow kuugal maako ko ada ko tageefo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Before coming to earth, Jesus ‚Äî the Word ‚Äî already existed. He was with God in the beginning, meaning He has always been in close relationship with the Father. More than that, He was fully God Himself. John also tells us that all things were created through Him, meaning Jesus was not a created being who appeared at some point in history, but the eternal Creator who was present before anything else existed.
elements.questionHint3: John says the Word was ‚Äôin the beginning‚Äô ‚Äî does that suggest Jesus had a starting point, or that He was already there before anything began? What else does John say about His role before creation?

elements.passageText1: <sup>15</sup>Shi ne surar Allah marar ganuwa, magƒÅji ne tun ba a halicci kome ba, <sup>16</sup>don ta gare shi ne aka halicci dukkan kome, abubuwan da suke a sama da abubuwan da suke a ∆ôasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa, ko kursiyai, ko masu mulki, ko masarauta, ko masu iko, wato, dukkan abubuwa an halicce su ne ta wurinsa, shi ne kuma makomarsu. <sup>17</sup>Shi ne a gaba da dukkan abubuwa, dukkan abubuwa kuma shi ne yake ri∆ôe da su.
elements.passageText2: <sup>15</sup>Almasihu shi ne kamannin Allah wanda ba a iya gani, shi ne ∆äan fari gaban dukan halitta. <sup>16</sup>Gama ta wurinsa ne aka yi kome da kome, abubuwan da suke a sammai da kuma a nan duniya, da abubuwan da ake gani da wa…óanda ba a iya gani, ko kujerun mulki, ko mulkoki, ko masu sarauta, ko ikoki. Dukan abubuwan da aka halitta, an halicce su ne ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa. <sup>17</sup>Almasihu shi ne a gaba da dukan kome, ta wurinsa ne kuma dukan halitta ta kasance a harha…óe.
elements.passageText3: <sup>15</sup>Kiristi shi ne surar da ake gani na Allah wanda ba a iya gani, …óan fari a kan dukan halitta. <sup>16</sup>Ta wurinsa aka halicci dukan abu, abubuwan da suke cikin sama da abubuwan da suke a duniya, wa…óanda ake gani, da wa…óanda ba a gani, ko kursiyoyi ko ikoki ko masu mulki ko hukumomi; an halicci dukan abu ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa ne. <sup>17</sup>Ya kasance kafin dukan abu, kuma a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harha…óe.
elements.passageText4: <sup>15</sup>Ngikku Almasiihu mo ngi‚Äôeten …óum go‚Äôo e ngikku Allah mo yi‚Äôataake. E mo …óon ko Allah taga koo …óume, o maw…óo dow koo …óume ko tagaa. <sup>16</sup>Barka maako Allah tagi koo …óume gon…óum dow e ley, ko yi‚Äôetee e ko yi‚Äôataake, hawti e malaa‚Äôika‚Äôen, e ginni, e koo …óume goodu…óum laamu e baw…óe. Koo …óume kanko tagi…óum, nden ngam maako koo …óume tagaa. <sup>17</sup>Almasiihu e …óon ko koo …óume tagee, barka maako koo …óume e yaha yeeso.
elements.passageText5: <sup>15</sup>He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation. <sup>16</sup>For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities‚Äîall things were created through him and for him. <sup>17</sup>And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce dukan abubuwa ‚Äî a sama da a ∆ôasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa ‚Äî an halicci su ta wurin Yesu, kuma dominsa. Ransa a halitta ya cika: babu abin da ya wanzu sai ta game da shi. Ayar 17 ta kai ma‚Äôana ta fi nisa, ta ce a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harha…óe. elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi‚Äôi koo …óume ‚Äî gon…óum dow e ley, ko yi‚Äôetee e ko yi‚Äôataake ‚Äî tagaa e Yeesu, e nder maako, e ngam maako. Kuugal maako nder tageefo wonnii haa timmi: walaa ko woni fuu ko tagiraayi laawol maako. Aaya 17 yaa…ìi dow non, wi‚Äôi nder maako koo …óume e yaha yeeso. ∆äum hollita Yeesu wanaa tan Tag…óo, ammaa kanko woni kadi mo riworaa tageefo hannde ‚Äî duuniyaaru e yaade yeeso e nder har…óondiral ngam maako. O wanaa ne…ó…óo wo…ó…óo fu…ó…óii ko woodi o ya…ì…ìi; o riworaa koo …óume e nder wakkati ngoo goongo.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus huutorii laa…ìi tati nder aaya 16 ‚Äî koo …óume tagaa ‚Äôe‚Äô, ‚Äòe nder‚Äô, e ‚Äôngam‚Äô Yeesu. ∆äume naatgol …ói tati …ói jokkondir…ói hollita e no o naatoyi nder tageefo, e noy aaya 17 yu…ì…ìitata miijo ngoo haa hannde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says that all things ‚Äî in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible ‚Äî were created by Jesus, through Jesus, and for Jesus. His role in creation was total: nothing came into existence apart from Him. Verse 17 then goes further, saying that in Him all things ‚Äôhold together‚Äô. This means Jesus is not just the originator of creation but its ongoing sustainer ‚Äî the universe continues to exist and cohere because of Him. He is not a distant figure who started things and stepped away; He is actively involved in holding everything together at this very moment.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses three prepositions in verse 16 ‚Äî all things were created ‚Äôby‚Äô, ‚Äòthrough‚Äô, and ‚Äôfor‚Äô Jesus. What does adding those three together suggest about the scope of His involvement in creation, and how does verse 17 extend that idea into the present?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Mala‚Äôika ya bayyana ma‚Äôanar sunan kai tsaye: Yesu yana nufin ‚Äôzai ceci mutanensa daga zunubansu‚Äô. Sunan kansa sanarwar aikin da zai yi ne. Yesu bai zo da farko a matsayin mai ‚Äôyantar da siyasa, ko malami na …óabi‚Äôa, ko mai yin mu‚Äôujizai ba ‚Äî ya zo ne don magance matsalar zunubi. Sunansa yana gaya mana cewa cetonsa daga zunubi shi ne tsakiyar manufar rayuwarsa, kuma an tsara wannan ceto an kuma sanar da shi kafin a haife shi.
elements.questionHint1: Mala‚Äôika ya bayyana dalilin sunan tare da kalmar ‚Äôdomin‚Äô ‚Äî wane matsala ta musamman ya ce Yesu zai zo don magance ta, kuma shin hakan yana ban mamaki ga ka/ki idan ka/ki yi la‚Äôakari da abin da mutane na iya tsammani mai ceto ya zo da yi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Mala‚Äôika hollitii maana innde nde tii…óu…óo: Yeesu nufata ‚Äôo hisnay yim…ìe maako daga hakke ma…ì…ìe‚Äô. Innde maako hoore mum woni tintinol kuugal maako. Yeesu waraa tan ko libiroowo siyaas, ko moodibbo laawol moralnaawol, koo ko wa…óoowo hay…ói ‚Äî o warii ngam wa…óde laawol e hakke. Innde maako hollita en hisnugol daga hakke ko faandaare nder nguurndam maako, e ngal faandaare nde ta∆¥anaa, e nde waajowaa …ìe ada ko o danyanaa.
elements.questionHint2: Mala‚Äôika on holliti daliil innde nde e wolde ‚Äôngam‚Äô ‚Äî ko woni ca…óeele he…ì…ìunde o wi‚Äôi Yeesu wari ngam wa…óde laawol e ma…ó…óe, e …óum hay…óina ma ko …ìe ndaari yim…ìe …ìen ke…ìugol ko hisnoo…óo wa…óata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The angel explains the name directly: Jesus means ‚Äôhe will save his people from their sins‚Äô. The name itself is a mission statement. Jesus did not come primarily as a political liberator, a moral teacher, or a miracle worker ‚Äî He came to deal with the problem of sin. His name tells us that rescue from sin is the central purpose of His life, and that this rescue was planned and announced before He was even born.
elements.questionHint3: The angel gives a reason for the name with the word ‚Äôfor‚Äô ‚Äî what specific problem does He say Jesus will come to solve, and does that surprise you given what people might have expected a Saviour to do?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Ayar 7 tana nuna cewa an haifi Yesu ta hanyar jiki ta yau da kullum ‚Äî an rufe shi da zane kamar kowace jariri kuma an kwantar da shi a kwamin dabbobi. Ya shiga duniya cikin rauni da talauci, ba cikin …óaukakar sama ba. Ayar 40 kuma tana gaya mana ya girma ya kawo ∆ôarfi, kuma ya cika da hikima a cikin lokaci. Wannan tsarin yaron …óan adam ne na yau da kullum: ci gaba, girma, da balagar hankali a hankali. Yesu bai haifa yana cikakke ko ya san dukan abubuwa a cikin gogewar sa ta mutum ba ‚Äî ya girma kamar yadda muke girma.
elements.questionHint1: Ayar 40 ta ce Yesu ya ‚Äôgirma‚Äô ya kuma ‚Äôkawo ∆ôarfi‚Äô ‚Äî shin wa…óannan kalmomi suna nuna wani abu da ya faru a lokaci …óaya ko a hankali a tsawon lokaci, kuma me hakan ke gaya maka game da ko yarinyarsa ta yi kama da tamu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Aaya 7 hollita Yeesu danyanaa e laawol …ìandu goonga ‚Äî o saawanaa nder wudere ko nii …ìii janan…óo koo gooto e o fukkaa nder to …óum wa…óanta marle nyamndu. O natti nder duuniyaaru e tampere e yimir…óo, hoto nder teddungal ngurleeji. Aaya 40 nden hollita o mawni o sembi…ói, e faamu heewii e makko e nder wakkati. ∆äum woni mbaydi …ìii ne…ó…óo goonga: yu…ì…ìere, mawningol, e mawnugol haa timmi e nder sahaa. Yeesu danyanaa wanaa timmi…óo koo andiraa…óo koo …óume e nder golle muu…óum ko ne…ó…óo ‚Äî o mawni ko nii mba…óaten mawnirta.
elements.questionHint2: Aaya 40 wi‚Äôi Yeesu ‚Äômawni‚Äô e ‚Äôsembi…ói‚Äô ‚Äî …óee kelmeeji hollita ko wa…ói hannde tan koo ko wa…ói e nder sahaa, e …óume …óum anndinta ma e to yarinyaaru maako wonndi e mee…óen?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Verse 7 shows that Jesus was born in the ordinary, physical way ‚Äî wrapped in cloth like any newborn infant and laid in a manger. He entered the world in vulnerability and poverty, not in heavenly splendour. Verse 40 then tells us He grew and became strong, and was filled with wisdom over time. This is the pattern of a normal human childhood: development, growth, and gradual maturation. Jesus was not born fully formed or all-knowing in His human experience ‚Äî He grew up just as we do.
elements.questionHint3: Verse 40 says Jesus ‚Äôgrew‚Äô and ‚Äôbecame strong‚Äô ‚Äî do those words suggest something that happened all at once or over time, and what does that tell you about whether His childhood was similar to ours?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Cewa Yesu ‚Äôya ji yunwa‚Äô yana nufin ya fuskanci ainihin yunwar jiki mai tsanani ‚Äî irin wadda ke zuwa bayan kwana arba‚Äôin ba tare da abinci ba. Ba misali ba ne; gaskiyar jiki ce. Mai iko na Allah ba zai ji yunwar jiki ba, amma Yesu ya ji. Hakan yana tabbatar da cewa jikin sa na mutum na ainihi ne kuma yana ∆ôar∆ôashin iyakokin jiki iri …óaya da namu: yana bu∆ôatar abinci, ya ji zafin rashinsa, kuma jikinsa ya raunana saboda gogewar. ∆äan adamtakarsa ba sutura ba ce ‚Äî na ainihi ne.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da yadda yunwa take ji bayan rasa abinci na cin abinci …óaya kawai ‚Äî me fuskantar ta bayan kwana arba‚Äôin ke gaya maka game da ko Yesu yana da ainihin jikin …óan adam mai bu∆ôatun jiki na gaskiya?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Wonde Yeesu ‚Äôweelo nangimo‚Äô hollita o nanngi weelo tigi …ìandu ‚Äî no nangirta ga…óa bal…óe 40 o nyaamaayi go…ó…óum. ∆äum wanaa kisal; ko goongawal …ìandu. Alla tan wanaa …ìandu waawataa nangugol weelo …ìandu, ammaa Yeesu nangi. ∆äum tabbintina …ìandu muu…óum ne…ó…óo goonga e …ìandu ngo fee…ìtonoo e ko ngo hi…ìi no mba…óaten e mee…óen nii: o haajii nyaamdu, o nanngi ca…óeele walugol e makko, e …ìandu maako tampii e golle …óen. Ne…ó…óam muu…óum wanaa wudere o saawii ‚Äî ko goonga.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e no weelo wa‚Äôi ga…óa …óa…ì…ìitugol nyaamdu hannde gooto tan ‚Äî …óume nangugol …óum ga…óa bal…óe 40 anndinta ma e to Yeesu ina jogii …ìandu ne…ó…óo goonga e haajuuji …ìandu goonga?
elements.sampleAnswer3: That Jesus ‚Äôwas famished‚Äô means He experienced real, intense physical hunger ‚Äî the kind that comes from forty days without food. This is not a metaphor; it is a physiological reality. A purely divine being would not experience bodily hunger, but Jesus did. This confirms that His human body was genuine and subject to the same physical limitations as ours: He needed food, He felt the pain of going without it, and His body was weakened by the experience. His humanity was not a costume ‚Äî it was real.
elements.questionHint3: Think about what hunger feels like after missing just one meal ‚Äî what does experiencing it after forty days tell you about whether Jesus had a real human body with real physical needs?

Gaskiyar cewa Yesu yana barci a cikin jirgin, ko a cikin babban hadari mai tsanani, yana gaya mana ya yi aiki sosai har ya gaji da ainihi. Bai yi kamar yana gaji ba; yana bu∆ôatar hutawa da gaske. elements.sampleAnswer2: ∆äaaningol woni go…ó…óo nder maandeeji maw…ói …ìurtinii…ói ne…ó…óam ‚Äî o hollita tampere …ìandu e haaju …ìandu e noppi. Goongawal wonde Yeesu …óaanii nder komiwal, haa e nder iyeende mawnde, hollita o golliino haa timmi o tampii goonga. O wa…óaayii hayka tampii; o haajii noppi goonga. elements.questionHint2: Ngam …óume aadee haajii …óaaningol, e …óume haaju …óaaningol anndinta ma e ko woni …ìandu ne…ó…óo ‚Äî mbaa…óaa mo tampataako goonga waawugol …óaaningol e nder iyeende ca…óende?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Sleep is one of the most fundamental marks of being human ‚Äî it reflects physical tiredness and the body‚Äôs need for rest. The fact that Jesus was asleep in the boat, even in the middle of a violent storm, tells us He had been working hard enough to be genuinely exhausted. He was not pretending to be tired; He actually needed rest. This small detail quietly but powerfully confirms that His humanity was authentic: He got tired just as we do.
elements.questionHint3: Why do human beings need to sleep at all, and what does needing sleep tell you about the kind of body a person has ‚Äî could someone who never truly got tired fall into a deep sleep during a dangerous storm?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya rubuta cewa sojoji ‚Äî ∆ôwararru wanda aikinsu shi ne tabbatar da mutuwa ‚Äî sun tabbatar da cewa Yesu ya riga ya mutu. Ba su da wata dalilin kara masa rai kuma suna da dalilin tabbatar da mutuwarsa. Gaskiyar cewa ya mutu da gaske yana da muhimmanci a matakai biyu. Da farko, yana tabbatar da cikakken …óan adamtakarsa: mai iko na Allah da ya bayyana kawai kamar …óan adam ba zai iya mutuwa ta gaskiya ba. Na biyu, yana tabbatar da cewa tashin matattu ya kasance tashin matattu na gaskiya daga mutuwa ta ainihi, ba farfadowa daga rashin sani ba. Yohanna yana ba da shaida ta ido cewa mutuwar ta ainihi ce, domin mai karatu ya iya dogara da cewa tashin matattu ya kasance na ainihi haka.

Aran…óe, o tabbintina ne…ó…óam muu…óum haa timmi: Alla tan wanaa …ìandu mo baylanaa ko nii ne…ó…óo waawataa maayde goonga. ∆äi…óa…ìe, o tabbintina umminaawal on wonnoo umminaawal goonga daga maayde goonga, hoto fayde daga …ìurtundiral. Yaya hokkata seede ne…ó…óo yi‚Äôu…óo wonde maayde nde goonga, ngam jooloo…óo on waawa hoolugo umminaawal kadi ko goonga.
elements.questionHint2: Sooji‚Äôen …ìen ngewi koy…óe wo…ì…ìe ngam hanyugol maayde ma…ì…ìe ‚Äî ngam …óume non …óum mawni wonde …ìe ngewaayi koy…óe Yeesu, e …óume …ìe tabbintoyii en goonga nde …ìe ye∆¥∆¥iri mo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John records that the soldiers ‚Äî professionals whose job was to ensure death ‚Äî confirmed that Jesus was already dead. They had no reason to spare Him and every reason to make sure. The fact that He was genuinely dead is significant on two levels. First, it confirms His full humanity: a divine being who merely appeared human could not truly die. Second, it establishes that the resurrection was a real resurrection from actual death, not a recovery from unconsciousness. John is providing eyewitness evidence that the death was real, so that the reader can trust that the resurrection was equally real.
elements.questionHint3: The soldiers broke the legs of the others to speed their deaths ‚Äî so why is it significant that they did not need to do this to Jesus, and what were they effectively confirming by leaving Him alone?

elements.sampleAnswer3: John opens his Gospel with a deliberate echo of Genesis ‚Äî ‚ÄòIn the beginning‚Äô ‚Äî and immediately places Jesus (the Word) there, before anything else existed. He then makes two statements in one breath: the Word was with God (pointing to a distinct, personal relationship) and the Word was fully God (pointing to a shared divine nature). This verse establishes from the outset that Jesus cannot be understood as merely a great man or even a lesser divine being ‚Äî He is fully God, and the rest of John‚Äôs Gospel is written to demonstrate what that means.
elements.questionHint3: John says the Word was both ‚Äôwith God‚Äô and ‚Äôwas fully God‚Äô ‚Äî do those two statements contradict each other, or could they together be pointing to something about Jesus that is more complex than a simple either/or?

Maimakon haka, ya yarda da tunanin sa‚Äôan nan ya aiki akan sa. Ya warkar da shanyayyen a matsayin hujja mai ganuwa cewa yana da ikon yin abin da Allah ka…óai zai iya yi: gafarta zunubi. Yeesu wi‚Äôataa o yaafoo hakke ngam laamu Allah, ko nii ardii…óo diina wa…óata; o wi‚Äôi o wa…óa e nder laamu maako hoore muu…óum. ∆≥amol maako ‚Äî ‚Äòko …ìuri hoyki?‚Äô ‚Äî woni tarko: tawa …ói …ói…ói ngalaa laawol ngam ne…ó…óo tan, wa…óugol gooto yeeso yim…ìe tabbintata laamu ngam wa…óugol go…ó…óo. elements.sampleAnswer3: The religious leaders correctly identify that only God can forgive sins ‚Äî and Jesus does not correct them on that point. Instead, He accepts the premise and then acts on it. He heals the paralysed man as visible proof that He has the authority to do what only God can do: forgive sins. Jesus is not claiming to forgive sins on God‚Äôs behalf, as a priest might; He is claiming to do it in His own right. His question ‚Äî ‚Äòwhich is easier?‚Äô ‚Äî is a trap: since both are impossible for a mere human, doing one publicly proves the authority to do the other. The miracle is His answer to their question.
elements.questionHint3: The religious leaders‚Äô logic was: only God forgives sins, therefore Jesus is blaspheming by claiming to do it ‚Äî but what does Jesus do next, and how does that action respond to the charge rather than simply arguing against it?

elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu wi‚Äôi o wanaa tan wakilaa…óo koo janngu…óo sa∆ôa daga Baaba on, ammaa o hollitii Baaba on e nanndi e makko haa timmi wonde yi‚Äôugol mo ko yi‚Äôugol Baaba on. ∆äum woni da‚Äôaawa hay…óunde sanne: o hollita to nda…ó…óa andugol no Alla wa‚Äôi ‚Äî nunaare muu…óum, goonga muu…óum, miijo muu…óum e maaybe, ta∆¥ugol muu…óum ngam tampugol ‚Äî mba ndaara Yeesu. elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus is claiming that He is not merely a representative or messenger of the Father, but that He so perfectly reveals and embodies the Father that seeing Him is seeing the Father. This is an extraordinary claim: it means that if you want to know what God is like ‚Äî His compassion, His honesty, His attitude to sinners, His willingness to suffer ‚Äî you look at Jesus. Jesus does not point people away from Himself to find God; He says that God is found in Him. It is a direct claim to share in the divine nature and identity of the Father.
elements.questionHint3: Philip wanted to be shown the Father as if Jesus and the Father were two entirely separate things to find ‚Äî why does Jesus find this question so bewildering, and what does His surprise tell you about how closely He sees Himself as connected to the Father?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Tambayar almajiran ta dace, kuma mu‚Äôujizin kansa yana ba da amsar. A Tsohon Alkawari, Allah ne ke umartar tekuna da iskoki ‚Äî halitta tana biyayya ga Mahaliccinta. Almajiran ba mamaki kawai ba ne suka yi; suka ‚Äôtsorata matu∆ôar tsoro‚Äô ‚Äî wani amsa da Littafi Mai Tsarki yawanci yake nuna a lokacin haduwa da Mai Iko. Mu‚Äôujizin yana nuna amsar: wannan shi ne Allah kansa cikin jikin mutum, wanda ya halicci teku kuma saboda haka yana da iko a kanta.

Tokku…ìemo …ìen ngalaa ko …ìe hay…óini ka≈ã≈ãe tan; …ìe ‚Äôkultori‚Äô ‚Äî jaabawol ngo e nder Biibal …óon holtiniree nde ne…ó…óo andira Allah. Hay…óu on hollita jaabawol: o‚Äôo woni Allah kanko nder nguurndam ne…ó…óo, mo tagi ma…ì…ìe e geeloo…ói ndiyam …óin e nder …óum jogii laamu dow ma…ó…óe.
elements.questionHint2: E nder Tawreeta, hol ko baylanaa e jogugol laamu dow ma…ì…ìe e iyeende? Dow fii …óu‚Äôum, …óume jaabawol no Markus yi…ói jooloo…óo yonkita daga ∆¥amol tokku…ìemo …ìen?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The disciples‚Äô question is the right one, and the miracle itself supplies the answer. In the Old Testament, it is God who commands the seas and the winds ‚Äî nature obeys its Creator. When Jesus rebukes the storm and it immediately calms, He is doing something that only God does. The disciples were not just impressed; they were ‚Äôoverwhelmed with fear‚Äô ‚Äî a response that in Scripture is typically prompted by an encounter with the divine. The miracle points to the answer: this is God Himself in human form, the one who made the sea and therefore has authority over it.
elements.questionHint3: In the Old Testament, who is described as having authority over the sea and storms? Knowing that, what conclusion does it seem like Mark wants the reader to draw from the disciples‚Äô question?

Maayde walaa baw…óe ngam tottugol mo. ∆äum hollita laamu maako dow maayde wanaa hollanaaki koo fam…óungol ‚Äî …óum woni nder yontere muu…óum. elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus does not say He can bring resurrection or that He knows the way to life ‚Äî He says He is the resurrection and the life. It is an identity claim, not just a capability claim. He then immediately backs it up by calling a man who had been dead for four days out of his tomb with a single command. Death had no power to resist Him. This tells us that Jesus‚Äô authority over death is not borrowed or limited ‚Äî it is intrinsic to who He is. And because He is the resurrection and the life, the promise He makes to believers ‚Äî that they will live even if they die ‚Äî rests on His own identity and power.
elements.questionHint3: Notice that Jesus says ‚ÄôI am the resurrection‚Äô rather than ‚ÄôI can perform resurrections‚Äô ‚Äî what is the difference between those two statements, and what does it suggest about where the power over death actually resides?

elements.answer3: People sometimes assume this means Jesus is in some way less than God ‚Äî a divine being, perhaps, but not fully God Himself. The Bible, however, uses ‚ÄôSon of God‚Äô to mean the opposite. When Jesus claimed to be the Son of God, his listeners understood it as a claim to be equal with God (John 5:18). ‚ÄôSon of‚Äô in this context conveys nature and identity, not origin or rank. Jesus is fully God ‚Äî not a lesser divine being, and not a created figure who became divine.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Marubuci ya ce domin an jarabci Yesu ta kowace hanya kamar yadda muke, yana iya jin tausayin rauni namu ‚Äî ba daga nesa ba, sai dai daga gogewar kansa. Hakan yana nufin lokacin da muke zuwa gare shi cikin gwagwarmayarmu, ba mu zuwa waje da wani wanda ya girgiza kuma ya ce ‚Äôban fahimci yadda hakan ke ji ba.‚Äô Muna zuwa waje da wanda ya fahimci, kuma ‚Äî abin muhimmanci ‚Äî ya shawo kanta ba tare da zunubi ba, wanda ke nufi ya san hanyar da za a bi.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da ko za ka fi so neman taimako daga wanda ya karanta kawai game da wata gogewa mai wahala ko daga wanda ya ta…ìa fuskantar ta da kansa ‚Äî ta yaya hakan ke shafi abin da wannan ayar take cewa game da Yesu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Windiijo on wi‚Äôi ngam Yeesu foondaama e koo ngole laawol no …óum foondirta‚Äôen, o waawata yurminanki‚Äôen tampu…ìe men ‚Äî hoto wo…ó…óere, ammaa daga golle muu…óum kanko. O wanaa Maw…óo Ardii…óo Diina mo riiwaa daga ca…óeele en nji‚Äôata; o anndi goonga ko woni nanngi jan foondaare daga nder. ∆äum hollita nde ngartaten e makko e nder fiyakugol men, en ngartataako e go…ó…óo mo renata wi‚Äôa ‚Äômi anndaa no …óum wa‚Äôi‚Äô. En ngartata e mo anndi, e ‚Äî ko mawni ‚Äî mo wa…óii e makko o wa…óaayi hakke, ko hollita o anndi laawol ngol e ma…ì…ìe.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e to mba…óata nda…ó…óa ballal daga mo yi…óii tan janngugol e ca…óeele ca…óende koo daga mo yo…ìii goonga e nder ma…ó…óe kanko ‚Äî noy …óum naatata e ko ndee aaya haalanta en e Yeesu?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The writer says that because Jesus was tempted in every way just as we are, He is able to sympathise with our weaknesses ‚Äî not from a distance, but from personal experience. He is not a high priest who is immune to the pressures we face; He knows what it is to feel the pull of temptation from the inside. This means that when we come to Him in our struggle, we are not coming to someone who shrugs and says ‚ÄôI don‚Äôt understand what that feels like.‚Äô We are coming to one who does understand, and who ‚Äî crucially ‚Äî came through it without sin, which means He knows the way through.
elements.questionHint3: Think about whether you would rather seek help from someone who has only read about a difficult experience or from someone who has actually been through it themselves ‚Äî how does that apply to what this verse is saying about Jesus?

elements.text3: The Holy Spirit is perhaps the least understood member of the Trinity. Many people think of Him as an impersonal force or a vague spiritual feeling. But the Bible is clear: He is a person ‚Äî the third person of the Trinity, alongside God the Father and God the Son. The Holy Spirit is also referred to in the Bible as the Spirit of Jesus (and of Christ and of God‚Äôs Son).

elements.passageText3: <sup>9</sup>Ku kam, ana mulkinku ba ta wurin mutuntaka ba, sai dai ta wurin Ruhu, in Ruhun Allah yana zaune a cikinku. In kuma wani ba shi da Ruhun Kiristi, shi ba na Kiristi ba ne.
elements.passageText4: <sup>9</sup>Ammaa onon on nga…óataa ko …ìalli moo…óon ngi…ói. On nga…óa ko Ruuhu Allah wi‚Äôi, to ni …óum goonga Ruuhu Allah e joo…óii nder …ìer…óe moo…óon. Koo moye to walaa Ruuhu Almasiihu:  Ruuhu Almasiihu e Ruuhu Allah fuu …óum go‚Äôo. , naa‚Äô Almasiihu jeyimo.
elements.passageText5: <sup>9</sup>You, however, are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God lives in you. Now if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, this person does not belong to him.

Haka kuwa yake, ba wanda zai gane tunanin Allah, sai dai Ruhun Allah. <sup>12</sup>Mu kuwa ba ruhun duniya muka kar…ìo ba, sai dai Ruhu wanda yake daga wurin Allah, domin mu gane abubuwan da Allah ya ba mu hannu sake. <sup>13</sup>Muna kuma gaya muku wa…óannan abubuwa, ba da kalmomin da ake koyarwa ta hikimar mutum ba, sai dai ta Ruhu, ta wurin bayyana abubuwan Ruhu ga wa…óanda suke na Ruhu. 2.13 ga wa…óanda suke na Ruhu: Da Helenanci an rubuta ‚Äúa cikin kalmomin ruhaniya‚Äù ne a nan. <sup>14</sup>Mutumin da ba Ruhu ne yake bi da shi ba, ba ya kar…ìar abubuwan Ruhun Allah, gama a gare shi wawanci ne. Ba kuwa zai iya gane su ba, gama ta wurin Ruhu ne ake ganewa. <sup>15</sup>Mutumin da Ruhu yake bi da shi kuwa, yana iya gwada kome amma ba wanda zai iya gwada shi.
elements.passageText3: <sup>10</sup>Amma Allah ya bayyana mana shi ta wurin Ruhunsa. <sup>11</sup>Wane ne a cikin mutane ya san tunanin mutum, in ba ruhun mutumin da yake cikinsa ba? Haka kuma ba wanda ya san tunanin Allah, sai dai Ruhun Allah. <sup>12</sup>Ba mu kar…ìi ruhun duniya ba, sai dai Ruhun da yake daga Allah, domin mu fahimci abin da Allah ya ba mu hannu sake. <sup>13</sup>Wannan shi ne abin da muke sanarwa, ba da kalmomin da muka koya ta wurin hikimar mutum ba, amma da kalmomin da Ruhu yake koyarwa, muna fa…óin gaskiya ta ruhaniya da kalmomin ruhaniya. <sup>14</sup>Mutumin da ba shi da Ruhu, ba ya yarda da abubuwan da suka fita daga Ruhun Allah, domin wauta ce a gare shi. Ba kuwa zai fahimce su ba, domin ta Ruhu ne ake fahimtarsu. <sup>15</sup>Mutumin ruhaniya yakan gwada dukan abubuwa, amma shi kansa ba ya ∆ôar∆ôashin shari‚Äôar mutum.
elements.passageText4: <sup>10</sup>Ammaa Allah hollii‚Äôen…óum laawol Ruuhu maako. Ngam Ruuhu maako e andi koo …óume haa e sirru maako luggu…óum. <sup>11</sup>Moye nder yim…ìe andata ko woni nder …ìernde ne…ó…óo to naa‚Äô ne…ó…óo on? Non maa, walaa andu…óo nyumo Allah sey ni Ruuhu Allah. <sup>12</sup>Ruuhu …óum ke…ìu…óen, naa‚Äô to duuniyaaru …óum ∆¥iwoyi; to Allah …óum ∆¥iwoyi, ngam anden caahu …óum o hokki‚Äôen. <sup>13</sup>E min meta dow …óu‚Äôum naa‚Äô nder haala ka faamu ne…ó…óo ekkitata, sey ni nder haala ka Ruuhu Allah ekkitinimin. E min ekkitina goodu…ìe Ruuhu Allah dow goonga Allah. <sup>14</sup>Ne…ó…óo mo walaa Ruuhu Allah, o ja…ìataa ko ∆¥iwoyi to Ruuhu Allah, ngam to maako …óum haa≈ãoore. O waawataa o faama…óum ngam o walaa Ruuhu Allah. <sup>15</sup>Goodu…óo Ruuhu Allah boo waaway faamuki koo …óume ko Ruuhu Allah ekkitata. Ammaa mo walaa Ruuhu Allah faamataa nyumo goodu…óo Ruuhu Allah.
elements.passageText5: <sup>10</sup>God has revealed these things to us by the Spirit, for the Spirit searches all things, even the deep things of God. <sup>11</sup>For who among men knows the things of a man except the man‚Äôs spirit within him? So too, no one knows the things of God except the Spirit of God. <sup>12</sup>Now we have not received the spirit of the world, but the Spirit who is from God, so that we may know the things that are freely given to us by God. <sup>13</sup>And we speak about these things, not with words taught us by human wisdom, but with those taught by the Spirit, explaining spiritual things to spiritual people. <sup>14</sup>Now the unbeliever does not receive the things of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishness to him. And he cannot understand them, because they are spiritually discerned. <sup>15</sup>The one who is spiritual discerns all things, yet he himself is understood by no one.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana gardama cewa kamar yadda ruhin mutum kawai ne ya san abin da ke gudana a ciki sa gaskiya, haka Ruhun Allah kawai ya san al‚Äôamuran Allah da zurfi. Ruuhu, andu…óo Allah e nder maako, o hollitta …óee kuu…óe goon…óin…ìe. Sa…ìaare nden Pool wi‚Äôi yim…ìe mo Ruuhu walaa ndaaran kuu…óe ruuhuuyankeeje bana haa≈ãoore: to walaa Ruuhu, a waawataa faamaade …óe tan. Ngam Kiristaajo, Ruuhu ko kanko wa…óata goonga Allah he…ìtoraade, laddeede, e feewniteede ‚Äî o mu…ó…óitora kelmii dow lahal ngam wahiyu nde yonki.

elements.passageText3: <sup>26</sup>Haka nan ma, Ruhu yana taimakonmu cikin rashin ∆ôarfinmu. Ba mu san abin da ya kamata mu ro∆ôa cikin addu‚Äôa ba, amma Ruhu kansa yana ro∆ôo a madadinmu da nishe-nishen da kalmomi ba sa iya bayyanawa.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yawancin Kiristoci suna fuskantar lokutan da suke ji masu kwarjini sosai, ko kuma mawuyaci, ko kuma gajiyayyu don yin addu‚Äôa ‚Äî ko kawai ba su san abin da za su ro∆ôa ba. Ta‚Äôaziyyar ba cewa addu‚Äôa ta zama sau∆ôi ba ce, bali gajiyawarmu a addu‚Äôa an lura da ita ‚Äî Ruhu yana …óaukar abin da ba za mu iya …óauka ba, kuma Allah, wanda ke binciken zukatanmu, yana fahimtar gaba …óayan abin da Ruhu ke bayyana a madadinmu.

elements.sampleAnswer2: Kiristanko‚Äôen …óuu…ó…ìe nanjata sahaaoji nde …ìe nanjata …ìe na ko …ìe maa nga…óa do‚Äôaare naa‚Äô ko …ìe maa ngulloto Allah ‚Äî walla tan andaa ko …ìe maa mbiya. elements.passageText1: <sup>8</sup>Wani an yi masa baiwa da koyar da hikima ta wurin Ruhu, wani kuma da koyar da sani ta wurin wannan Ruhu. <sup>9</sup>Wani kuwa an yi masa baiwa da bangaskiya ta wurin Ruhun nan, wani kuma baiwar warkarwa ta wurin Ruhun nan, <sup>10</sup>wani kuma yin mu‚Äôujizai, wani kuma annabci, wani kuma baiwar rarrabe ruhu da aljani, wani kuma iya harshe iri iri, wani kuma fassara harsuna.
elements.passageText2: <sup>8</sup>Wani an ba shi baiwar koyar da hikima ta wurin Ruhu Mai Tsarki, wani kuma an yi masa baiwar koyar da sani ta wurin wannan Ruhu. <sup>9</sup>Wani kuma an yi masa baiwar bangaskiya ta wurin Ruhun nan, wani kuma baiwar warkewa ta wurin Ruhun nan, <sup>10</sup>wani kuma baiwar yin ayyukan ban mamaki, wani kuma annabci, wani kuma iya bambanta tsakanin ruhohi, wani kuma iya magana da harsuna dabam-dabam, wani kuma iya fassara harsuna.
elements.passageText3: <sup>8</sup>Ga wani, an ba shi sa∆ôon hikima ta wurin Ruhu, ga wani baiwar sa∆ôon sani ta wurin wannan Ruhu guda. <sup>9</sup>Ga wani bangaskiya ta wurin wannan Ruhu, ga wani kuma baiwar warkarwa ta wurin wannan Ruhu guda. <sup>10</sup>Ga wani yin ayyukan banmamaki, ga wani annabci, ga wani baiwar rarrabe ruhohi, ga wani baiwar magana da harsuna dabam-dabam, ga wani har wa yau iya fassarar harsuna.
elements.passageText4: <sup>8</sup>Ruuhu …óum, kanjum hokkata go…ó…óo dokkal wolde faamu, go…ó…óo boo dokkal wolde andal. <sup>9</sup>Kanjum hokkata go…ó…óo dokkal hoolaaki Allah, nden go…ó…óo boo dokkal yam…óitinki nyaw…ìe, <sup>10</sup>go…ó…óo kadin dokkal wa…óuki fii kay…óinii…óum, go…ó…óo wa…óuki annabaaku, go…ó…óo boo mbawka feerootirki hakkunde Ruuhu Allah e ginni, go…ó…óo boo mbawka wa…óirki do‚Äôaare …óem…óe …óe …ìe andaa, go…ó…óo boo mbawka hokkuki ma‚Äôana …óem…óe …óen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>For to one is given a message of wisdom through the Spirit, and to another a message of knowledge by the same Spirit, <sup>9</sup>to another faith by the same Spirit, and to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit, <sup>10</sup>and to another performance of miracles, to another prophecy, and to another discernment of spirits, to another different kinds of tongues, and to another the interpretation of tongues.

elements.answer1: Mutane da yawa suna ganin Triniti tana da rikitarwa - har ma da Kiristoci. Kalmar da kanta ba ta bayyana a cikin Littafi Mai Tsarki ba, wanda zai iya ∆ôara wa rashin tabbas. Abin da Littafi Mai Tsarki ke koyarwa shi ne akwai Allah …óaya, wanda yake wanzuwa a matsayin mutum uku: Uba, ∆äa, da Ruhu Mai Tsarki. Wa…óannan ukun sun bambanta - ba kawai ‚Äôyanayi‚Äô uku ko abin rufe fuska ba ne da Allah ke sawa a lokuta daban-daban - duk da haka Allah …óaya ne, ba uku ba. Triniti ba sabani ba ne, amma asiri ne da ya fi gaban cikakken fahimtar …óan adam. Abin da za mu iya fa…ói shi ne cewa Uba, ∆äa, da Ruhu Mai Tsarki suna da irin wannan yanayin allahntaka kuma suna aiki tare a cikin duk abin da Allah yake yi.
elements.answer2: Yim…ìe heew…ìe ina njiya Trinit√© ina jii…ìii ‚Äî hay Kerecee‚Äôen. Konngol ngol e hoore mum fee√±ataa e nder deftere nde, …óum ina waawi …ìeydude ≈ãakkeende hoolaare. Ko Biibal ngal janngini ko, Alla gooto ina woodi, ina woodi no yim…ìe tato nii: Baaba, ∆Åi…ó…óo e Ruuhu Ceniii…óo. ∆äee tati ina ceerti ‚Äî …óe ngonaa tan ‚Äômode‚Äô tati walla masi≈ãaaji …ói Alla …ìoornotoo e sahaaji ceertu…ói ‚Äî kono ko Alla gooto, wonaa tati. Trinit√© wonaa luural, kono ko sirlu mo ne…ó…óo waawaa faamde timmu…óo. Ko mbaaw-…óen wiyde ko Baaba, ∆Åi…ó…óo, e Ruuhu Ceniii…óo ina njogii sifaa gooto, ina ngollodoo e kala ko Alla wa…óata.
elements.answer3: Many people find the Trinity confusing ‚Äî even Christians. The word itself doesn‚Äôt appear in the Bible, which can add to the uncertainty. What the Bible does teach is that there is one God, who exists as three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. These three are distinct ‚Äî they are not simply three ‚Äômodes‚Äô or masks that God wears at different times ‚Äî yet they are one God, not three. The Trinity is not a contradiction, but it is a mystery beyond full human understanding. What we can say is that the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit share the same divine nature and work together in everything God does.

elements.text1: Acikin kowa ce tattaunawa da wanda ke da kokwanto cewar akwai Allah. Maganar halitta ta kan yawan tasowa. Acikin wannan binciken ba lallai ya yiwu a iya magance duka mahawara game da halitta ba amma zamu duba bayani halitta bisa ga Littafi Mai Tsarki.
elements.text2: Koo nde yim…ìe …óon mboldata dow woodu…óo Allah koo walaa, tefere dow tagu duuniyaaru …óon yalta. Nder jannge …óu‚Äôum, min mawaata min kauna koo …óume dow tagu, ammaa ko …ìuri maandaade woni ko Allah e hoore mum wolwi dow mu‚Äôum. Ngam majjum, nder jannge …óo, min njaa haa habaru mo Deftere Allah hokkata ‚Äî nden ka kadi e woodi ko wolwata ko a tamyaayi.
elements.text3: Questions about creation come up whenever people discuss whether God exists. There is far more to explore here than one study can cover, but what matters most is what God Himself says about it. So in this study we go to the Biblical account ‚Äî and it has more to say than you might expect.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Dukan ayoyin biyu suna nuna Allah a matsayin Halicci na gaskiya kuma asalin komai. Farawa 1:1 ya fa…ói wannan a sarari cikakke ‚Äî ‚ÄòA farkon farko, Allah ya halicci sama da ∆ôasa‚Äô ‚Äî ba tare da barin wuri ga tunanin cewa halitta ta faru ba tare da Shi ba ko kafin Shi. Ishaya 45:12 ya ∆ôara bayani kai tsaye: Allah Yana magana a mutum na farko, yana cewa ‚ÄôNi ne wanda ya halicci duniya‚Äô kuma ‚Äôhannuwana ne suka shimfi…óa sammai‚Äô. Bai ba wani iko aikin halitta ba; Ya yi shi da Kansa, da cikakken iko ‚Äî har zuwa inda Ya ba taurari umarni. Hoton da aka nuna shine na Allah wanda shi ne mai halitta na mutum, mai ikon mallaka, kuma da niyya wanda ya kasance asalin dukan abin da ya wanzu.

elements.sampleAnswer2: Si…ó…óe …ói…ói …óuye …óon hollita Allah hono Tagotoowo go…ó…óo e fu…ó…óorde ko fuu. Fu…ó…óoode 1:1 …óon wi‚Äôa …óum tii…ónde e kala nganaaka ‚Äî ‚ÄòDaga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi‚Äôetee…óo, Allah tagi dow e ley‚Äô ‚Äî walaa …óoyngal ngal ko taguuji wa…óii ko yanaatii Allah koo …ìuri maako. Ishaya 45:12 …óon …ìura …óum hollitaade tii…óunde: Allah …óon wolwoo e hoore mum, wi‚Äôa ‚ÄôMi wa…ói leydi‚Äô nden ‚ÄôJuu…óe am …óe njiilay dow‚Äô. O hokkii go…ó…óo …óum kuugal tagu; O wa…ói ka√±um e hoore mum, bee kala laamu ‚Äî haa nde O hokki taaraabiiji …ói umaruje. Hoyre nde hollirtee woni hoyre Allah tagotoowo, jom laamu, nde wa…ói koo …óume bee nuun…óal.
elements.questionHint2: Nder Ishaya 45:12, Allah …óon huutoo wolde ‚ÄôMi‚Äô e ‚ÄôJuu…óe am‚Äô ‚Äî ko woni ko wolde …ói…óe …óe jolnitii…óe …óe hollata dow no O neli hoore mum nder tagu, nden kadi dow ko o waawaa wa…óde …óum bee ballal go…ó…óo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Both passages present God as the sole Creator and originator of everything that exists. Genesis 1:1 states this simply and absolutely ‚Äî ‚ÄòIn the beginning God created the heavens and the earth‚Äô ‚Äî leaving no room for creation to have happened without Him or before Him. Isaiah 45:12 adds a note of personal directness: God speaks in the first person, saying ‚ÄôI made the earth‚Äô and ‚Äômy own hands stretched out the heavens‚Äô. He did not delegate creation to another power; He made it Himself, with full authority ‚Äî even giving orders to the stars. The overall picture is of a God who is the personal, sovereign, and intentional originator of all that exists.
elements.questionHint3: In Isaiah 45:12, God uses the words ‚ÄôI‚Äô and ‚Äômy own hands‚Äô ‚Äî what does that personal, direct language suggest about how involved He was in the act of creation, and whether He needed anyone else‚Äôs help?

elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. <sup>2</sup>The Word was with God in the beginning. <sup>3</sup>All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.

elements.passageText1: <sup>16</sup>don ta gare shi ne aka halicci dukkan kome, abubuwan da suke a sama da abubuwan da suke a ∆ôasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa, ko kursiyai, ko masu mulki, ko masarauta, ko masu iko, wato, dukkan abubuwa an halicce su ne ta wurinsa, shi ne kuma makomarsu. <sup>17</sup>Shi ne a gaba da dukkan abubuwa, dukkan abubuwa kuma shi ne yake ri∆ôe da su.
elements.passageText2: <sup>16</sup>Gama ta wurinsa ne aka yi kome da kome, abubuwan da suke a sammai da kuma a nan duniya, da abubuwan da ake gani da wa…óanda ba a iya gani, ko kujerun mulki, ko mulkoki, ko masu sarauta, ko ikoki. Dukan abubuwan da aka halitta, an halicce su ne ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa. <sup>17</sup>Almasihu shi ne a gaba da dukan kome, ta wurinsa ne kuma dukan halitta ta kasance a harha…óe.
elements.passageText3: <sup>16</sup>Ta wurinsa aka halicci dukan abu, abubuwan da suke cikin sama da abubuwan da suke a duniya, wa…óanda ake gani, da wa…óanda ba a gani, ko kursiyoyi ko ikoki ko masu mulki ko hukumomi; an halicci dukan abu ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa ne. <sup>17</sup>Ya kasance kafin dukan abu, kuma a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harha…óe.
elements.passageText4: <sup>16</sup>Barka maako Allah tagi koo …óume gon…óum dow e ley, ko yi‚Äôetee e ko yi‚Äôataake, hawti e malaa‚Äôika‚Äôen, e ginni, e koo …óume goodu…óum laamu e baw…óe. Koo …óume kanko tagi…óum, nden ngam maako koo …óume tagaa. <sup>17</sup>Almasiihu e …óon ko koo …óume tagee, barka maako koo …óume e yaha yeeso.
elements.passageText5: <sup>16</sup>For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities‚Äîall things were created through him and for him. <sup>17</sup>And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yahaya 1:1-3 yana gaya mana cewa ba komai aka halicci ba tare da Yesu ba ‚Äî Shi ba halittu bane amma shi ne wanda ta wurinsa dukan halitta ta fito. Kolosiyawa 1:16-17 ya ∆ôara bayani: dukan abubuwa an halicci su ba kawai ta wurinSa ba amma daSa kuma dominSa ‚Äî ma‚Äôana cewa dukan tsarin halitta yana da Yesu a matsayin asalinsa da makasudi. Aya ta 17 ta ∆ôara cewa Shi ‚Äôyana a gaba da dukan abubuwa‚Äô kuma ‚Äôa cikinSa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harha…óe‚Äô, wato duniya tana ci gaba da wanzuwa saboda ∆ôarfinSa na kiyayewa na ci gaba. Ga mutane da yawa da suke tunanin Yesu a matsayin malami ko mai warkarwa kawai, wannan hoton Shi a matsayin Haliccia kuma Mai Kiyaye dukan abubuwa yana canza yadda suke fahimtar wane ne Shi.
elements.questionHint1: Kolosiyawa 1:16 yana cewa an halicci komai ba kawai ‚Äôta wurin‚Äô Yesu ba amma kuma ‚ÄôdominSa‚Äô ‚Äî me yake nufi ga halitta ya kasance an yi shi dominSa, kuma yadda wannan ya kwatanta da yadda kuke tunaninSa a al‚Äôada?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya 1:1-3 …óon hollita en ko walaa …óume tagaa ko yanaatii Yeesu ‚Äî O waayi tagu…óo, ammaa woni mo tagu fuu wa…óiraayi laawol mum. Kolosiyanko‚Äôen 1:16-17 …óon …ìura …óum maantinde: koo …óume tagaa ko kanko ka√±um tag…óum, nden ngam maako ka√±um koo …óume tagaa ‚Äî nde wa…ói kala nganaaka tagu…óo renndoroo Yeesu ko fu…ó…óorde mum e makaa…óe mum. Si…ó…óo 17 …óon …ìura wi‚Äôde o e …óon ko koo …óume tagee, nden barka maako koo …óume e yaha yeeso, woni duuniyaaru nde e jokki e yaha ngam baaw…óe maako nde hollinatnoo koo adde. Ngam yim…ìe heew…ìe miijotoo…ìe Yeesu hono jannginoo…óo koo naa garanii…óo, hoyre nde hollita mo hono Tagotoowo e Garoowo koo …óume …óon waylita no …ìe mbaawa annditaade ko kanko woni.
elements.questionHint2: Kolosiyanko‚Äôen 1:16 …óon wi‚Äôa koo …óume tagaa ko e Yeesu ko ‚Äôngam maako‚Äô ka√±um ‚Äî ko woni ma‚Äôana tagu wa…óaama ngam Yeesu, nden no …óum seedintini e no a miijortoo Maako koo adde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John 1:1-3 tells us that nothing was created apart from Jesus ‚Äî He was not a created being but the one through whom all creation came into existence. Colossians 1:16-17 deepens this further: all things were created not just through Him but by Him and for Him ‚Äî meaning the entire created order has Jesus as both its origin and its goal. Verse 17 then adds that He is ‚Äôbefore all things‚Äô and that in Him all things ‚Äôhold together‚Äô, meaning the universe continues to exist and cohere because of His ongoing sustaining power. For many people, thinking of Jesus primarily as a teacher or healer, this picture of Him as the Creator and Sustainer of all things significantly changes how they understand who He is.
elements.questionHint3: Colossians 1:16 says everything was created not just ‚Äôthrough‚Äô Jesus but also ‚Äôfor‚Äô him ‚Äî what does it mean for creation to have been made for Jesus, and how does that compare with the way you normally think about Him?

elements.question1: Farawa 1:2 da Zabura 104:30 suna bayyana menene game da rawar Ruhu Mai Tsarki wajen halitta? Me yake nufi cewa mutumin uku uku na Triniti duka sun shiga wajen halitta?
elements.question2: Ko Fu…ó…óoode 1:2 e Jabuura 104:30 kollata dow kuugal Ruuhu Cenii…óo nder tagu? Nden kadi ko woni ma‚Äôana ko ne…ó…óo tati (Tatiraajo) fuu njaadi nder tagu ngun?
elements.question3: What do Genesis 1:2 and Psalm 104:30 reveal about the Holy Spirit‚Äôs role in creation? What does it mean that all three persons of the Trinity were involved?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Farawa 1:2 yana sanya Ruhu Allah daidai a farkon ‚Äî yana shawagi bisa ruwayen da ba su da siffa, babu komai kafin Allah ya fara yin halitta cikin tsari. Hoton yana nuna kasancewar mai aiki da mai shiri, wanda ya shiga daga farko. Zabura 104:30 yana nuna cewa aikin Ruhu na halitta ba iyakancewa ne ga aiki na farko kawai ba: lokacin da Allah Ya aika da RuhuNSa, ana halicci halittu kuma ∆ôasa tana sabuntawa. Ruhu yana shiga cikin halitta ta ci gaba da sabunta, ba kawai a farkon abin da ya faru ba. Shigar mutane uku ukun Triniti ‚Äî Uba, ∆äa, da Ruhu ‚Äî wajen halitta yana gaya mana cewa halitta ba aikin wani memba …óaya ba ne da ya yi shi a shi ka…óai, amma aiki ha…óin kai na dukan Allahanci. Duniya ba ta fito daga wani …ìangare na Allah kawai ba amma daga cikar wanda Allah yake.
elements.questionHint1: Farawa 1:2 yana nuna Ruhu yana nan daidai a farkon, kafin komai ya tsara ‚Äî kuma Zabura 104:30 yana nuna Shi yana shiga wajen kawo rayuwa sabuwa. Wa…óannan lokuttan biyu tare suna nuna menene game da fa…óin shiga Ruhu wajen halitta?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Fu…ó…óoode 1:2 …óon …óoyngana Ruuhu Allah haa fu…ó…óorde tigi ‚Äî o neli dow ndiyam ko walaa sifaa, baaw…óe, ko fuu …ìaawo Allah fu…ó…óata tagu e diidol. Hoyre nde hollirtee …óon hollita kasandiral mo jar…óinaa…óo, mo neli laawol maako daga fu…ó…óorde. Jabuura 104:30 …óon hollita ko kuugal Ruuhu nder tagu …óon walaa taraani e waato fu…ó…óoode tan: nde Allah neli Ruuhu Mum, taguuji tagaa nden leydi …óon hisantaa koy. Ruuhu …óon neli laawol maako nder tagu e saabu maare, walaa e waato fu…ó…óoode tan. Tawa Tatiraajo fuu ‚Äî Baaba, ∆Åii, e Ruuhu ‚Äî njaadi nder tagu …óon hollita en tagu waayi ko taa…ìal go…ó…óo e hoore mum wa…óaa e hoore mum, ammaa taa…ìal ha…óii…óel ngel …óuurondiri e Allah. Duuniyaaru nde yaltaay e gure go‚Äôo nder Allah, ammaa e he…ìi-he…ìi maako fuu.
elements.questionHint2: Fu…ó…óoode 1:2 …óon hollita Ruuhu e …óon ko koo …óume tagee bi‚Äôetee…óo, ko koo fuu sifaaka ‚Äî nden Jabuura 104:30 …óon hollita mo neli laawol maako nder wuurde sabu…óe ya…óde. Ko woni ko dakiika …ói…ói …óuye …óe heewi hollitaade dow hebitaare ko Ruuhu neli laawol maako nder tagu?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Genesis 1:2 places the Spirit of God at the very beginning ‚Äî moving over the formless, empty waters before God speaks creation into order. The image suggests a hovering, active presence, ready and involved from the outset. Psalm 104:30 shows that the Spirit‚Äôs creative work is not limited to the initial act: when God sends His breath (Spirit), living creatures are created and the ground is renewed. The Spirit is involved in ongoing creation and replenishment, not just in the original event. The involvement of all three persons of the Trinity ‚Äî Father, Son, and Spirit ‚Äî in creation tells us that creation was not the work of one member acting alone, but a unified act of the whole Godhead. The world did not come from one dimension of God but from the fullness of who God is.
elements.questionHint3: Genesis 1:2 shows the Spirit present at the very start, before anything was formed ‚Äî and Psalm 104:30 shows Him involved in bringing new life into being. What do those two moments together suggest about the scope of the Spirit‚Äôs involvement in creation?

elements.question3: It‚Äôs worth reading the whole passage before answering the question below‚Ä¶
elements.answer3:
1In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. 2Now the earth was without shape and empty, and darkness was over the surface of the watery deep, but the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the water. 3God said, ‚ÄòLet there be light.‚Äô And there was light! 4God saw that the light was good, so God separated the light from the darkness. 5God called the light ‚Äôday‚Äô and the darkness ‚Äônight.‚Äô There was evening, and there was morning, marking the first day.

6God said, ‚ÄòLet there be an expanse in the midst of the waters and let it separate water from water.‚Äô 7So God made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above it. It was so. 8God called the expanse ‚Äôsky.‚Äô There was evening, and there was morning, a second day.

9God said, ‚ÄòLet the water under the sky be gathered to one place and let dry ground appear.‚Äô It was so. 10God called the dry ground ‚Äôland‚Äô and the gathered waters he called ‚Äôseas.‚Äô God saw that it was good. 11God said, ‚ÄòLet the land produce vegetation: plants yielding seeds and trees on the land bearing fruit with seed in it, according to their kinds.‚Äô It was so. 12The land produced vegetation‚Äîplants yielding seeds according to their kinds, and trees bearing fruit with seed in it according to their kinds. God saw that it was good. 13There was evening, and there was morning, a third day.

14God said, ‚ÄòLet there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate the day from the night, and let them be signs to indicate seasons and days and years, 15and let them serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to give light on the earth.‚Äô It was so. 16God made two great lights‚Äîthe greater light to rule over the day and the lesser light to rule over the night. He made the stars also. 17God placed the lights in the expanse of the sky to shine on the earth, 18to preside over the day and the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw that it was good. 19There was evening, and there was morning, a fourth day.

20God said, ‚ÄòLet the water swarm with swarms of living creatures and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky.‚Äô 21God created the great sea creatures and every living and moving thing with which the water swarmed, according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. 22God blessed them and said, ‚ÄòBe fruitful and multiply and fill the water in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth.‚Äô 23There was evening, and there was morning, a fifth day.

24God said, ‚ÄòLet the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: cattle, creeping things, and wild animals, each according to their kind.‚Äô It was so. 25God made the wild animals according to their kinds, the cattle according to their kinds, and all the creatures that creep along the ground according to their kinds. God saw that it was good.

26Then God said, ‚ÄòLet us make humankind in our image, after our likeness, so they may rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air, over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over all the creatures that move on the earth.‚Äô 27God created humankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them, male and female he created them.

28God blessed them and said to them, ‚ÄòBe fruitful and multiply! Fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air and every creature that moves on the ground.‚Äô

31God saw all that he had made‚Äîand it was very good! There was evening, and there was morning, the sixth day.

2:1The heavens and the earth were completed with everything that was in them. 2By the seventh day God finished the work that he had been doing, and he ceased on the seventh day all the work that he had been doing. 3God blessed the seventh day and made it holy because on it he ceased all the work that he had been doing in creation.
