elements.text3: It is possible to study the Bible and know it well and, yet, miss the point of why it was written ‚Äî to show us how we can have a right relationship with God.

elements.text1: Bayanai da muka samu ta yarda zamu san Allah wurin ba da gaskiya ga Ubangiji Yesu Almasihu, zai kasance a taimako ga wa…óan da ba su gano hakan ba da kansu.

elements.text3: The following explanation of how we can know God personally, by believing on the Lord Jesus Christ, may be helpful to those who have yet to make that discovery for themselves.

elements.text1: Idan ka/kin riga ka/kin kasance mai bi (da ko ∆¥ar Allah) ko ka gane mai hakan ke nufi to ka rubuta abinda ka gane kafin zuwa kashi na biyu na wannan litafin Tsage Gaskiya
elements.text2: To a laataki tawon Almasiihuujo (malla …ìi…ó…óo Allah) ‚Äî malla a andaa ko …óum fassirta sam ‚Äî janngu …óee binndi bako a yaada haa Ge…ìal ∆äi…óabal deftere Goonga Udditaama.
elements.text3: If you are not already a Christian (or child of God) ‚Äî or even uncertain what that really means ‚Äî do read these notes before moving on to Part Two of Truth Unlocked.

elements.text2: Ngam …óume min ngalaa tawon nder jokkondiral dirmungal e Allah?
elements.text3: Why are we not already in a right relationship with God?

elements.text1: A yayin da Allah ya halicci duniya. Ya yi mu cikin sura da kamannin sa saboda mu samu kyakyawar dangantaka da shi mu kuma kasance masu girmama da …óaukaka shi. Amma zunubi ya …ìata wannan zumunci da Allah ya tsara. A lokacin da Adamu da Hauwa‚Äôu suka yi rashin biyaya ga Allah cikin lambun Eden
elements.text2: Nde Allah tagi duuniyaaru, O wa…ói en dow nanndola Makko ngam en mbaawa he…ìugo jokkondiral e Makko, en nguurna ngam teddingo Mo. Ammaa hakke bonni jokkondiral ngal Allah yi…ói ngal, nde Aadama e Hawwa‚Äôu calii nanngugo Allah nder Jarne Eden.
elements.text3: When God created the world, He made us in His likeness so that we might relate with Him and live to glorify Him. But sin spoiled the relationship which God intended, when Adam and Eve disobeyed Him in the Garden of Eden.

elements.text1: Mun kasance magada zunubi acikin rayuwar mu

elements.text3: God is absolutely holy and pure. He cannot tolerate sin and that has become a barrier between God and man.

elements.text1: Kasancewar Allah mai adalci ne, shi yasa ma ba zai iya yi halin ko in kula da zunubin mu ba. Dole ne zunubi ya fuskanci hukunci.
elements.text2: Ngam Allah ko aadiliijo, O waawataa weebugo hakkeeji mee…óen meere. Doole njo…ìdi wooda ngam majjum.
elements.text3: As God is just, He cannot simply ignore our sin. There has to be a punishment for it.

elements.text1: Duk da haka, Ya kasance mai ∆ôauna da jinkai. ∆òaunarsa ta kasance mai girma wanda ya yi ∆ôo∆ôarin yarda zamu dawo gare shi maimakon ya hukunta mu. Ya kuma samu yiwuwar hakan ta wurin aiko da ∆äansa, Yesu, Ya zo ya yi rayuwa a wannan duniya a matsayin mutum ya kuma mutu a maimakon mu.
elements.text2: Ammaa O jom-en yi…óde e jom-en hinney. Yi…óde Makko mawni sanne haa O …óon yi…ói fottinde en e Makko naa‚Äô o jukka en. O wa…ói …óum laataki bee nulgol ∆Åi…ó…óo Makko, Yeesu, warugo nguurna dow lesdi bana ne…ó…óo, O maayi boo nder pelle mee…óen.
elements.text3: However, He is certainly loving and merciful. His love is so great that He longs to restore us rather than punish us. He made that possible by sending His Son, Jesus, to come to live on earth as a human being and to die in our place.

elements.text3: The Bible tells us: ‚Äò;But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.‚Äô; ‚Äî Romans 5:8

elements.text1: Akwai manufar cewa Allah zai hukunta mu?
elements.text2: ∆äum …óon fassira Allah jukkataa en naa?

elements.text1: Ana nufin cewa baza mu fuskanci hukunci Allah ba, hakan zai yiwu ne kawai idan mu za…ìi mu kar…ìi gafarar Allah. Zamu iya tambayar Allah ya yafe mana zunuban mu in har mun ba da gaskiya cewar Yesu Almasihu ya mutu a madadin mu maimakon mu fuskancin Zunuban mu.
elements.text2: ∆äum …óon fassira en nji‚Äôataa kiita Allah ‚Äî ammaa to en cu…ìii ja…ìugo yaafuye Allah tan. En mbaaway andugo Allah O yaafoo en to en nja…ìii dow Yeesu Almasiihu yo…ìanii en njo…ìdi hakkeeji mee…óen.
elements.text3: It means that we will not have to face God‚Äôs punishment ‚Äî but only if we choose to receive God‚Äôs forgiveness. We can ask God to forgive us if we believe that Jesus Christ paid the penalty for our sins.

elements.text1: An yi bayanin kar…ìa gafarar zunuban mu a matsayin ‚Äôsake/maye haihuwa‚Äô cikin Littafi Mai Tsarki. Samun gafarar zunubai kasancewa ne cikin fara sabuwar rayuwa a matsayin mai bin Yesu.
elements.text2: Ja…ìugo yaafuye hakkeeji mee…óen …óon fassira nder Deftere Allah bana ne…ó…óo ‚Äôdanyaa kadi‚Äô. ∆äum woni fud…óoode nguurndam kesam bana tokkoowo Yeesu.
elements.text3: Receiving the forgiveness of our sins is described in the Bible as being ‚Äòborn again‚Äô. It is the start of a new life as a follower of Jesus.

elements.text1: Menene zai faru idan muka yi burus ko muka ∆ôi kar…ìar Yesu?
elements.text2: ∆äume feewata to en cu…ìii calagol ja…ìugo Yeesu?
elements.text3: What happens if we decide not to accept Jesus?

elements.text1: Hakan na nufin mun ‚Äô∆ôi‚Äô yarda da shirin Allah na ceto, kuma zamu fuskance hukunci. Hakan zai faru ne lokacin da Yesu zai dawo wannan duniya, saboda dawowarsa dawowa ce ta shari‚Äôa da kuma hukunci - ga duk wa…óan da suka ∆ôi kar…ìa gafarar da Allah ya bayar.
elements.text2: To en wi‚Äôii ‚ÄòOo‚Äôo‚Äô haa feere hisre Allah, enen e koo‚Äôe mee…óen en jukkete. ∆äum feewata wakkati Yeesu lorti haa lesdi, ngam O waray kiita ‚Äî e jukkugo ‚Äî koo moye mo ja…ìaayi yaafuye mo Allah hokki.
elements.text3: If we say ‚ÄòNo‚Äô to God‚Äôs rescue plan, we will be punished ourselves. That will happen when Jesus returns to earth, for He will come to judge ‚Äî and punish ‚Äî everyone who has not accepted God‚Äôs offer of forgiveness.

elements.text1: Hukunci ne mai tsanani kuma dauwamamme wanda zai raba duk wanda suka ∆ôi ka…ìar gafarar Allah har abada. (Basu ba Allah har abada)
elements.text2: ∆äum wonay jukkungo …ìurngo mettugo, woni seerndego e Allah haa abada.
elements.text3: It will be the most awful punishment of being separated from God forever.

elements.text1:
Littafi Mai Tsarki ya fa…óa mana: ‚Äò;‚Ä¶ Wanda kuwa bai ba da gaskiya ba, an riga an yi masa hukunci, domin bai gaskata da sunan maka…óaicin ∆äan Allah ba.‚Äô;

Yahaya 3:18
elements.text2: Deftere Allah …óon wi‚Äôa en: ‚Äò;...amma koo moye mo hoolaaki, kiita feewi dow mum jonta ngam o hoolaaki innde ∆Åii Allah bajjo.‚Äô; ‚Äî Yaya 3:18
elements.text3: The Bible tells us: ‚Äò;‚Ä¶but whoever does not believe stands condemned already because they have not believed in the name of God‚Äôs one and only Son.‚Äô; ‚Äî John 3:18

elements.text1: Menene nake bu∆ôata domin zama Krista?
elements.text2: ∆äume mi haani wa…óugo ngam mi laata Almasiihuujo?
elements.text3: What do I need to do to become a Christian?

elements.text1: Zaka/ki iya zama mai bi Yesu ka/ki kuma samu gafara zunubai ta wurin
elements.text2: A waaway laataago tokkoowo Yeesu nden a he…ìa yaafuye hakkeeji ma fuu bee:
elements.text3: You can become a follower of Jesus and have all your sins forgiven by:

elements.text1:
• Yarda da cewar baka/ki iya kiyaye ka‚Äôidoji/sharu…óan Allah ba kuma ka/kin cancanci hukunci Allah,
  • Gaskata cewar Yesu ne ka…óai zai iya ceton ka/ki daga hukuncin Allah,
  • Furta abubuwan da ka/kika yi na sa…ìaawa Allah a cikin rayuwar ka/ki da kuma barin su gami da juya musu baya,
  • ka/ki kuma za…ìi yin rayuwa ta farantawa Allah rai a matsayin ka/ki na mai bin Yesu.
elements.text2:
• ja…ìugo wondugo a …óowaaki doosije Allah nden a fotii e jukkungo,
  • hoolugo wondugo Yeesu tan waaway hisnugo ma diga jukkungo Allah,
  • hunttugo ko nga…ó-…óa ko woo…óaa nder nguurndam ma e tuubugo majjum ‚Äî woni, waylitaago diga majjum,
  • su…ìugo nguurnndam bel…óam Allah bana tokkoowo Yeesu.
elements.text3:
• accepting that you have not kept God‚Äôs laws and deserve to be punished,
  • believing that only Jesus can rescue you from God‚Äôs punishment,
  • confessing the wrong things in your life and repenting of them ‚Äî that is, turning away from them,
  • deciding to live to please God as a follower of Jesus.

elements.text1: Idan ka/kin shirya bin wannan matakan, zaka/ki iya yin haka ta amfani da wannan adu‚Äôa
elements.text2: To a sirriyi wa…óugo …óee njaabawuuji, a waaway wa…óugo …óum jonta bee huutorgol ndee du‚Äôaure.
elements.text3: If you are ready to take those steps, you can do so now by using the following prayer.

elements.text1:
Ina neman ahuwa game da dukan rayuwa da ta wuce da kuma ta yanzu, wacce ba ta akan tsari. Yanzu na tuba daga zunubai na. Na gode domin aiko da Yesu ya mutu domin a maimako na da kuma zunubai na, saboda in tsira daga hukuncin da na cancanta.

Ina son in yi rayuwa domin in gamshi Yesu in kuma mai da shi jigon rayuwa ta - Ya kuma kasance Sarki na. Ina ro∆ôo ka yafe min dukkan zunubai na ka kar…ìe ni matsayin …óanka/∆¥arka.

Ina ro∆ôonka ka cika ni da Ruhunka don ya taimake ni yin sabuwar rayuwa Amin
elements.text2:
Barkama-ka am mawnin…óo Allah,

Mi…óo ninsita sanne ngam koo …óume nder nguurndam am, ko saali e ko joni, ko laa…ìaa.

Mi…óo tuubi hakkeeji am jonta.

Mi yetta Ma ngam a nulgii Yeesu maayi nder pelle am, ngam hakkeeji am, ngam hisnugo am diga jukkungo ngo mi fotii e mum.

Mi…óo yi…ói nguurnndam am mbelna Yeesu, mi wa…óa Mo boo caka nguurndam am ‚Äî O laata Lam…óo am.

Min rooki, yaafu am hakkeeji am fuu nden nja…ìaa am bana …ìi…ó…óo Ma. Hebbina am bee Ruhu Ma Cenii…óo ngam wallugo am nguurn mi nguurndam kesam.

Aamiina
elements.text3:
Dear Father God,

I am very sorry for everything in my life, past and present, which is not right.

I now repent of my sin.

Thank You for sending Jesus to die in my place, for my sins, in order to save me from the punishment which I deserve.

I want to live to please Jesus and to make Him the centre of my life ‚Äî to be my King.

Please forgive all my sins and accept me as Your child. Please fill me with Your Holy Spirit to help me live a new life.

Amen

elements.text1: Idan har ka/ki yi wannan adu‚Äôa da gaske, Allah ya kar…ìe k/ki a …óa/∆¥a. Dukkan zunuban ka/ki na da da na yanzu da ma nan gaba - an yafe su duka
elements.text2: To a wa…óii ndee du‚Äôaure bee …ìernde laa…ìnde, Allah ja…ìii ma bana …ìi…ó…óo Makko. Hakkeeji ma fuu ‚Äî ko saali, ko joni, e ko warata ‚Äî yaafaama.
elements.text3: If you have been sincere with this prayer, God has received you as His child. All your sins ‚Äî past, present and future ‚Äî are forgiven.

elements.text1: To sai kuma me? Ka/ki na bu∆ôatar yin girma wannan kuwa zai iya faruwa ne yayin da ka/ke rayuwa da sauran masu bi cikin al‚Äôamuran yau da kullum in da akan yi biciken Littafi Mai Tsarki da kyau kuma bisa ka‚Äôida. Saboda da haka, mataki na farko shine ha…óa kan ka/ki da wata ikkilisiyar Almasihu da kuma masu bi da zasu ha…óuwa da masu bi da zasu taimaka maka/ki.
elements.text2: Nden …óume tokki jonta? A nannge mawna bana Almasiihuujo, nden …óum feewata tan to a …óon hawra bee Almasiihu‚Äôen wo…ì…ìe wakkati fuu haa Deftere Allah janngintee bee hakkillo. Ngam majjum, jaabawu ma arandewu woni hawtaago bee ekklisiya mo nokkuure ma nden e Almasiihu‚Äôen …ìe mbaaway wallugo ma.
elements.text3: And what now? You will need to grow as a Christian and that can only happen if you meet regularly with other Christians where the Bible is taught carefully. Therefore, the first step is to link with a local church and with Christians who will be able to help and support you.

sections.heading1: Wannan Nazarin Yaya Yake Da Amfani?

blocks.title1: Mutanen da suka fara sanin Kiristanci
blocks.body1: Idan kana da …óan tarihin coci ko babu, wannan nazari wuri ne mai kyau don fara. Ba ya …óauka cewa kana da ilimin da ya gabata. Yana bi ta tushen imani na Kirista ‚Äî wanda Allah yake, wanda Yesu yake, abin da Littafi Mai Tsarki ke fa…óa game da zunubi da ceto ‚Äî kuma yana bayyana kowannensu daga Littafi Mai Tsarki kansa. Yi shi a farantin ka, kuma kada ka ji tsoron rubuta tambayoyin da suka taso maka yayin da kake ci gaba.
blocks.body2: So a jogii keeweendi e laa…ìal kanisa maa maa walaa, jokkondiral nguu woni dow dow ngam fu…ótaade. Walaa miijo maa fam…óin…óo. Njokku e daarde imaan Kirista ‚Äî hol Allah woni, hol Iisaa woni, ko Deftere Allah wi'i e dow hakke e kisiindi ‚Äî kala gootal …ìurata e nder Deftere Allah keewnde. Gollal e laawol maa, maa hulaa winditde …óem…óe ndiyam …óe ngarii maa e nder yeeso.
blocks.body3: If you have little or no church background, this study is a good place to start. It doesn't assume any prior knowledge. It works through the foundations of Christian belief ‚Äî who God is, who Jesus is, what the Bible says about sin and salvation ‚Äî and explains each one from the Bible itself. Take it at your own pace, and don't be afraid to write down the questions that occur to you as you go.

blocks.title1: Mutanen da suka girma a cikin al'ummar Kiristanci
blocks.title2: Rew…ìe …ìe ndarii e nder laa…ìal Kirista
blocks.title3: People who grew up in Christian circles
blocks.body1: Idan ka kasance a kusa da coci mafi yawan rayuwarka, kila za ka gano cewa ba ka ta…ìa tsayawa don tambayi tambaya ta asali ba: shin na gane kuma na gaskata abin da nake tsammanin na gaskata? Kana san harshen, kana san labarun, kila ma kana san Littafi Mai Tsarki da kyau. Wannan nazari an tsara shi don taimaka maka bincika tushen a hankali da gaskiya ‚Äî ba don damunka ba, amma don ba ka wani abu ∆ôar∆ôashin ∆ôafa.
blocks.body2: So a woodnoo e haa…ìre kanisa e nder duu…ìi maa fof, a waawi yiyde ya a haalanaa tammbinde …óem…óe ndiyam mawnde: mbele mi fam…óii e mi yettiima ko mi miijii mi yettii? A anndi …óemngal, a anndi tindir…óe, kono a waawi fawnude Deftere Allah. Jokkondiral nguu dirtinaa ngam wallitde maa tortoraade e daarde …ìur…óe e goongal ‚Äî walaa ngam hu…ìindude maa, kono ngam hokkaade maa ko feewi ngam nji…ótaade dow.
blocks.body3: If you have been around church for most of your life, you may find that you have never stopped to ask a basic question: do I actually understand and believe what I think I believe? You know the language, you know the stories, you may even know the Bible well. This study is designed to help you examine the foundations carefully and honestly ‚Äî not to unsettle you, but to give you something solid to stand on.

blocks.title1: Mutanen da ke koyar da wasu
blocks.body1: Idan kana tafiya tare da wani a imaninsa ‚Äî a matsayin mai ba da shawara, shugaban ∆ôungiya ∆ôarama, ko aboki ‚Äî wannan nazari yana ba ka hanya mai tsari don bi ta tushen imani na Kirista tare. Nazarin takwas sun rufe ∆ôasar da ta fi muhimmanci: wanda Allah yake, mutum da aikin Almasihu, Ruhu Mai Tsarki, halitta, zunubi, gicciye, tashin matattu, da amsarmu. Kowane babi yana ∆ôarewa da tambayoyin tunani na sirri wa…óanda ke ∆ôarfafa tattaunawar da ta fi zurfi.
blocks.body2: So a njokkinii go…ó…óo e nder imaan muu…óum ‚Äî ko kalfineejo, ko hooreejo jam'iyya …ìur…óo, maa ko tawanteejo ‚Äî jokkondiral nguu hokkata maa laawol dirtinaa…óo ngam gollondirde e daarde imaan Kirista. Jokkondiraaji jeetati …ìee mawnoroo leydi nde …ìuri fawde: hol Allah woni, maw…óo e gollal Almasiihu, Ruuhu Cenii…óo, yamiroore, hakke, ladde, nguyka, e jaabawol amen. Kala esde jokkoo e …óem…óe miijor…óe …óe njii…ótinirta haala mawngo.
blocks.body3: If you are walking alongside someone else in their faith ‚Äî as a mentor, a small group leader, or a friend ‚Äî this study gives you a structured way to work through the foundations of Christian belief together. The eight studies cover the ground that matters most: who God is, the person and work of Christ, the Holy Spirit, creation, sin, the cross, the resurrection, and our response. Each chapter ends with personal reflection questions that naturally prompt deeper conversation.

blocks.body1: Wannan nazari yana aiki da kyau a matsayin nazarin sirri. Yi ta kowane babi a farantin ka, karanta sassan Littafi Mai Tsarki, rubuta amsoshin ka ga tambayoyin, kuma yi tunani a kan tambayoyin tunani a ∆ôarshe. Babu saurin da ya dace ‚Äî wasu babi na iya …óaukar mintuna ashirin, wasu na iya …óaukar tsawo idan tambaya ta tsayar da kai.
blocks.body2: Jokkondiral nguu gollortoo e nder yiyngel maa. Gollal kala esde e laawol maa, ja≈ãgu cu…óoli Deftere Allah, windu jaabaaji maa e …óem…óe ndiyam, maa miijoro e …óem…óe miijor…óe e nder yiynde. Walaa faango feew…óo ‚Äî kala esde waawa wa…óde minooje capan…óe …ói…ói, wiy…ìe waawa wa…óde bal…óe keewnde so …óemngal ndiyam neldii maa.
blocks.body3: This study works well as a personal study. Work through each chapter at your own pace, read the Bible passages, write your answers to the questions, and meditate on the reflection questions at the end. There is no right speed ‚Äî some chapters may take twenty minutes, others may take longer if a question stops you in your tracks.

blocks.body1: Nazarin kuma yana aiki da kyau a cikin ∆ôungiya ∆ôarama. Za ku iya bi ta kowane babi tare daga farko, karanta sassan kuma tattauna tambayoyin a matsayin ∆ôungiya. A madadin haka ‚Äî kuma wannan yawanci shine hanyar da ta fi amfani ‚Äî kowane mutum yana bi ta babi da kansa da farko, rubuta amsoshinsa, sannan ∆ôungiyar ta taru don tattauna abin da suka gano. Kowace hanya tana aiki. An tsara tambayoyin don ∆ôarfafa tunani na gaske, ba kawai amsoshi masu kyau ba.
blocks.body2: Jokkondiral nguu gollortoo e nder jam'iyya …ìur…óo. A waawi gollondirde kala esde fu…ótaade e fu…ótorde, ja≈ãgude cu…óoli e haaldude …óem…óe ndiyam ko jam'iyya. Maa tawon ‚Äî kono ko oon laawol …ìuri rewde ‚Äî kala gooto golloto e esde e yiyngel muu…óum faa adii, windu jaabaaji muu…óum, haa jam'iyya nguurdana haalde ko njiyii. Laawol kala gollortoo. ∆äem…óe ndiyam dirtinaa ngam firnaade miijo goongal, walaa ko jaabaaji …ìur…ói tii…óde.
blocks.body3: The study also works well in a small group setting. You could work through each chapter together from scratch, reading the passages and discussing the questions as a group. Alternatively ‚Äî and this is often the most rewarding approach ‚Äî each person works through the chapter individually first, writes their answers, and then the group comes together to discuss what they found. Either way works. The questions are designed to prompt genuine reflection, not just right answers.

sections.heading1: Shugabannin ∆òungiya da Masu Ba Da Shawara

blocks.title1: Yin aiki tare da shugaba ko mai ba da shawara
blocks.title3: Working with a leader or mentor
blocks.body1:
Yana iya yin babban bambanci idan akwai wanda ke jagorantar ka ta cikin nazarin ‚Äî shugaban ∆ôungiya, fasto, ko Kirista mai ∆ôarin gogewa wanda zai iya taimaka maka tunani a kan amsoshin ka da tambayoyinka. Watakila su ne suka ba ka shawara game da wannan nazari a farkon farko.

A ∆ôarshen kowane babi, ma…ìallin Raba yana baka damar aika amsoshin ka na rubutu kai tsaye ga shugabanka ko mai ba da shawara ‚Äî ta WhatsApp, imel, ko kowane app a wayarka. Wannan yana ba su taga zuwa ga tunaninka kafin ku hadu, kuma yana sa tattaunawar ta zama mai ∆ôarin arzi∆ôi.
blocks.body2:
Waawi wa…óde go…ó…óo fewjande maa e nder jokkondiral nguu ‚Äî hooreejo jam'iyya, moodi, maa Kirista fam…óin…óo …ìuri mo waawi wallitde maa miijorde e jaabaaji maa e …óem…óe maa ndiyam. Tawon ko kanko addani maa jokkondiral nguu e fu…ótorde.

E nder yiynde kala esde, biyol Feernu hokkata maa laawol neldude jaabaaji maa windaa…ói tii…ó…ói e hooreejo maa maa kalfineejo maa ‚Äî e dow WhatsApp, imel, maa mbiyundi kese e nder telefon maa. Ngam hokka …ìe tiitoonde e miijo maa …ìaawo tawandiroyde, maa wa…ótu haala …ìurnde mo∆¥∆¥ude.
blocks.body3:
It can make a real difference to have someone guiding you through the study ‚Äî a group leader, a pastor, or a more experienced Christian who can help you think through your answers and questions. Perhaps they are the one who recommended this study to you in the first place.

At the end of each chapter, the Share button lets you send your written answers directly to your leader or mentor ‚Äî via WhatsApp, email, or any other app on your phone. This gives them a window into your thinking before you meet, and makes for much richer conversation.

blocks.body1: Kowane babi yana da nau'ukan tambayoyi biyu. Tambayoyin Littafi Mai Tsarki an ha…óa su da sassan takamaiman ‚Äî suna tambayar ka ka duba da kyau abin da rubutun ke fa…óa. Tambayoyin tunani a ∆ôarshe sun fi na sirri ‚Äî suna tambayar abin da babi ke nufin maka musamman.
blocks.body2: Kala esde woodii …óem…óe ndiyam …ói…ói. ∆äem…óe ndiyam Deftere Allah dirtinaa e cu…óoli …ìur…ói ‚Äî tammbinirta maa yiylude e himminde ko binndol wi'i. ∆äem…óe miijor…óe e nder yiynde …ìuri sikkitaare ‚Äî tammbinirta maa ko esde ngoo firi maa e nder yiyngel maa.
blocks.body3: Each chapter has two kinds of questions. The Bible questions are tied to specific passages ‚Äî asking you to look carefully at what the text says. The reflection questions at the end are more personal ‚Äî asking what the chapter means for you specifically.

blocks.body1: A cikin duk nazarin za ka sami katunan da ke da launi mai duhu a kai da taken Fahimtar Kuskure ta Kowa. Wa…óannan suna magance kalmomi ‚Äî kamar alheri, tuba, ko imani ‚Äî wa…óanda Kiristoci ke amfani da su koyaushe amma da yawa ana fahimtar su ba daidai ba. Yi …óan lokaci don tunani game da abin da kake fahimta da kalmar, sannan tabba Gano don ganin …óan bayani.
blocks.body2: E nder gollal jokkondiral nguu fof a waawi ka…óe …ìal…óe e nder …ìal…óe Fam…óaari ∆Åur…ói Yiyde. ∆äee …óem…óe njii…ótinirta ‚Äî ko nimre, tawbee, maa imaan ‚Äî …óe Kiristee…ìe ndokkira kala sahaa kono …óe fam…óaa keewaado. Wa…ótu sahaa miijorde ko a fam…óii e nder …óemngal ngaal, haa hi…ìu Yiy ngam yiyde tiitoonde …ìurnde fam…óude.
blocks.body3: Throughout the study you will encounter darker-coloured cards headed Common Misunderstanding. These address words ‚Äî like grace, repentance, or faith ‚Äî that Christians use all the time but that are often misunderstood. Take a moment to think about what you understand by the word, then tap Find out to see a short clarification.

intro1: An tsara wa…óannan bayanan ne don taimaka wa shugabannin rukuni da masu ba da shawara su sami mafi kyawun amfani daga kowace babi - duka ga mutanen da suke jagoranta da kuma kansu. Ba su zama madadin yin aiki ta hanyar abubuwan da kanka ba da farko. Manyan shugabanni su ne wa…óanda suka yi aiki ta hanyar tambayoyin da kansu kafin su taimaki wasu su yi hakan.

∆òir∆ôiri wuri mai aminci don gaskiya. Tambayoyin da ke cikin wannan binciken an tsara su ne don su haifar da tunani na gaske, ba kawai amsoshi daidai ba. Aikinku shine ku sa ∆ôungiyar ta ji da…óin fa…óin abin da take tunani a zahiri, ba abin da suke tunanin Kirista ya kamata ya fa…óa ba.
Kada ku yi gaggawar yin shiru. Wasu daga cikin mafi kyawun lokutan nazarin rukuni suna faruwa bayan tambaya ta taso kuma babu wanda ya yi magana na …óan lokaci. Ku guji sha‚Äôawar cike kowane shiru.
Ku kula da mutumin da ya yi shiru. Wani lokaci mafi mahimmanci abin da ke faruwa a cikin …óakin shine tare da mutumin da ya fa…ói ka…óan. Ku duba tare da su daga baya.
intro2: ∆äee …óoo binndan…óe mba…óaama ngam wallude ardii…ìe pelle e ja≈ãnginoo…ìe he…ìde ko …ìuri heewde e kala tonngoode ‚Äì wonande yim…ìe …ìe …ìe ardii …ìee e ko‚Äôe ma…ì…ìe. ∆äi ngonaa lomto gollal e nder ge…óe …óee e hoore maa ko adii. Ardii…ìe …ìur…ìe mo∆¥∆¥ude ko …ìeen …ìe ngollii e nder naamne …óee e hoore mum hade ma…ì…ìe wallude wo…ó…ìe wa…óde noon.

Sosde nokku kisal ngam nuun…óal. Naamne gon…óe e ndee …óoo wi…óto mba…óaama ngam ustude miijo goonga, wonaa tan jaabawuuli laa…ìtu…ói. Golle mon ko wa…óde fedde nde ina he…ìa kisal haa heewi ngam haalde ko …ìe cikkata tigi rigi, wonaa ko …ìe cikkata ko Kerecee‚Äôen poti haalde.

Woto njaawno silsil. Won e sahaaji …ìur…ói mo∆¥∆¥ude e nder ja≈ãde fedde ina mba…óa caggal nde naamnal jippii tawa hay gooto haalataa ko ina wona le∆¥∆¥an…óe see…óa. Reento yi…óde hebbinde kala silsil.
Reento ne…ó…óo jahoowo dee∆¥…óo. Heen sahaaji ko …ìuri tee≈ãtude e ko wa…óata e nder suudu nduu ko e ne…ó…óo wiy…óo ko …ìuri fam…óude. ∆≥eewto e ma…ì…ìe caggal …óuum.
intro3: These notes are designed to help group leaders and mentors get the most out of each chapter ‚Äì both for the people they are leading and for themselves. They are not a substitute for working through the material yourself first. The best leaders are those who have worked through the questions personally before they help others doing the same.

Create a safe space for honesty. The questions in this study are designed to prompt real reflection, not just correct answers. Your job is to make the group feel safe enough to say what they actually think, not what they think a Christian is supposed to say.
Don‚Äôt rush the silences. Some of the best moments in a group study happen after a question lands and nobody speaks for a few seconds. Resist the urge to fill every silence.
Watch for the person who goes quiet. Sometimes the most significant thing happening in the room is with the person who says the least. Check in with them afterwards.

chapters.keyPoints1:
Wannan babi ya kafa tushen binciken: cewa ba sai mun yi hasashen yadda Allah yake ba, domin ya bayyana kansa. Muhimman motsin su ne (1) wanzuwar Allah kamar yadda halitta da Littafi Mai Tsarki suka tabbatar, (2) halin Allah - mai tsarki, madawwami, marar canzawa, yana ko‚Äôina, da kuma ∆ôauna - da kuma (3) nuna ∆ôaunarsa ta ∆ôarshe wajen aiko da ∆äansa.

Sashe na ∆ôarshe kan yadda muke san Allah yana ∆ôaunarmu (Romawa 5:8; 1 Yahaya 4:9‚Äì10) yana da matu∆ôar muhimmanci: ∆òaunar Allah ba ta da wani ra‚Äôayi na zahiri ba ne amma aiki ne na zahiri, wanda aka yi kafin mu cancanci hakan ko kuma mu ro∆ôe shi.
chapters.keyPoints2:
Ndee …óoo deftere ina tabitina mbaydi jannde ndee: wonde en ngalaa haaju e teskaade no Alla wayi, sabu ko kanko fee√±nini hoore makko. Dille maw…óe …óee ko (1) woodde Alla no tagoore ndee e Binndi …óii ceedtorii nii, (2) sifaa Alla ‚Äî ceni…óo, duumotoo…óo, mo waylaaki, gon…óo e kala nokku, e yi…óde ‚Äî e (3) hollirde yi…óde makko laa…ìtunde e neldude ∆Åiyiiko.

Ta∆¥re sakkitiinde e dow ho:w God us lo (5). 1 Yuhanna 4:9‚Äì10) ina teskaa: Yi…óde Alla wonaa miijo ngo alaa …óo haa…ói kono ko golle laa…ìtu…óe, ∆¥ettaa…óe ko adii nde en poti …óum walla naamnaade …óum.
chapters.keyPoints3:
This chapter establishes the study‚Äôs foundation: that we do not have to guess what God is like, because He has revealed Himself. The key movements are (1) the existence of God as evidenced by creation and Scripture, (2) the character of God ‚Äî holy, eternal, unchanging, omnipresent, and loving ‚Äî and (3) the decisive demonstration of His love in sending His Son.

The final section on how we know God loves us (Romans 5:8; 1 John 4:9‚Äì10) is particularly important: God‚Äôs love is not an abstract sentiment but a concrete act, taken before we deserved it or asked for it.
chapters.pastorals1:
Mutane da yawa suna zuwa irin wannan karatu suna …óauke da siffar Allah mai nisa, mai bu∆ôata, ko kuma kawai ba a san ta ba. Tambayar farko ‚Äî ‚ÄòYaya Allah yake da gaske?‚Äô ‚Äî ta cancanci …ìata lokaci a kai. Kada ku yi gaggawar wuce shi don zuwa ga nassoshin Littafi Mai Tsarki.

Nassin daga Zabura 139:7‚Äì10 (halartar Allah a ko‚Äôina) na iya zama abin damuwa ga wasu kuma yana kwantar da hankali ga wasu. Duk halayen biyu suna da daraja a bincika. Tambayar ta tambayi ∆ôungiyar ta ambaci wacece take a gare su ‚Äî ku …óauki hakan da muhimmanci.
chapters.pastorals2:
Heew…ìe ina ngara e ja≈ãde hono ndee ina njogii natal Alla ngal wo…ó…óaani, ngal naamnii, walla ngal laa…ìaani tan. Naamnal udditngal ‚Äî ‚ÄòHol no Alla wayi tigi rigi?‚Äô ‚Äî ina foti wa…óde heen sahaa. Hoto yaawa ∆¥akkugo …óum ngam he…ìugo binndi Bibel.

Bindi Jabuura 139:7‚Äì10 (Goo≈ãgaaku Allah haa kala nokkuure) waawan laataago …ìillaaji ngam wo…ì…ìe, boo …óon semmbi…óina wo…ì…ìe. Reactions …ói…ói …óee fof ina poti yuurniteede. Naamnal ngal naamndii fedde nde innde hol nde woni e ma…ì…ìe ‚Äî ∆¥etta …óum no haanirta nii.
chapters.pastorals3:
Many people come to a study like this carrying an image of God that is distant, demanding, or simply vague. The opening question ‚Äî ‚ÄòWhat is God really like?‚Äô ‚Äî is worth spending time on. Don‚Äôt rush past it to get to the Bible passages.

The passage from Psalm 139:7‚Äì10 (God‚Äôs omnipresence) can be unsettling for some and comforting for others. Both reactions are worth exploring. The question asks the group to name which it is for them ‚Äî take that seriously.
chapters.watch1: üëÅÔ∏è Ku kula da mutanen da suka yarda da wanzuwar Allah a hankali amma ba su ta…ìa ha…óa hakan da ∆ôaunar da Allah yake musu ba. Matsar da ‚ÄôAllah yana wanzuwa‚Äô zuwa ‚ÄôAllah yana ∆ôaunata‚Äô ba abu ne da aka saba yi ba, kuma wannan babi yana ∆ôo∆ôarin tabbatar da hakan.
chapters.watch2: üëÅÔ∏è Yeewtu yim…ìe ja…ìoo…ìe woodde Alla e hakkillaaji mum en kono …ìe mee…óaa jokkondirde …óum e yi…óde Alla e ma…ì…ìe. Diwgol ‚ÄòAlla ina woodi‚Äô feewde e ‚ÄòAlla ina yi…ói mi‚Äô wonaa huunde wee…ìnde, ndee…óoo tonngoode ina etoo wa…óde …óum.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who intellectually accept God‚Äôs existence but have never personally connected that with God‚Äôs love for them. The move from ‚ÄòGod exists‚Äô to ‚ÄòGod loves me‚Äô is not automatic, and this chapter tries to make it.

chapters.keyPoints3:
This chapter makes two essential and complementary claims about Jesus: that He is truly human and truly God. Both matter. His humanity means He genuinely understands our experience. His deity means His death has infinite weight and His words carry ultimate authority.

The Common Misunderstanding callout on ‚ÄòSon of God‚Äô addresses a very widespread confusion: that the title implies Jesus is somehow less than God. Ensure the group understands that in its Biblical context it means the opposite.

Hebrews 4:15 ‚Äî that Jesus was tempted in every way, yet without sin ‚Äî is both a proof of His humanity and a source of comfort. It should not be rushed past.
chapters.pastorals3:
Some in the group may have friends or family of other faiths who honour Jesus as a prophet or teacher but reject His divinity. The material in this chapter gives them something to think through carefully, not as ammunition for arguments, but as an invitation to look at the evidence more closely.

The miracle passages (calming the storm, raising Lazarus) can prompt questions about whether miracles are credible. Acknowledge the question honestly: the chapter is not trying to prove miracles philosophically, but to show what the eyewitnesses recorded and what that implies about who Jesus is.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who are comfortable with Jesus as a moral example or spiritual teacher, but resist the claim that He is God. This chapter is designed to press that question gently but clearly.

chapters.keyPoints3:
The Holy Spirit is often the least understood person of the Trinity. This chapter establishes three things: (1) the Spirit is a person, not a force or feeling; (2) He is given to every believer, not just some; and (3) His ultimate purpose is to glorify Christ (John 16:14).

The distinction between gifts (1 Corinthians 12) and fruit (Galatians 5) is worth dwelling on. Gifts are distributed sovereignly by the Spirit for the benefit of others; fruit is the character produced over time in every believer by the Spirit‚Äôs work.

The Common Misunderstanding callout on ‚Äòthe Trinity‚Äô addresses one of the most common sources of confusion in Christian theology. Be prepared for this to open wider questions.
chapters.pastorals3:
Different people in the group may come from very different backgrounds when it comes to the Spirit: some may have had charismatic experiences they are unsure about; others may be sceptical of anything experiential. The chapter tries to hold the ground carefully ‚Äî affirming the Spirit‚Äôs genuine and present work, without prescribing what that must look or feel like.

The reflection question about spiritual gifts can be particularly fruitful in a group setting. People often discover gifts they hadn‚Äôt named, or are encouraged by others who can see gifts in them that they can‚Äôt see in themselves.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who equate the Spirit‚Äôs work entirely with dramatic visible manifestations, or conversely those who are so cautious that they effectively discount Him altogether. The chapter aims to hold a Biblical centre.

chapters.keyPoints3:
This chapter covers the Biblical account of creation: who was involved (all three persons of the Trinity), what was made, how it was made (by God‚Äôs word alone), and why ‚Äî for God‚Äôs glory, and for relationship with Him.

The unique dignity of human beings made in the image of God (Genesis 1:27) is central. It distinguishes us from the rest of creation and grounds both our value and our responsibility. The two stewardship passages (Genesis 1:26; 2:15) point to creation care as a genuine theological concern, not a secular add-on.

The Genesis 1 reading callout gives the group a chance to read the full creation account together ‚Äî worth encouraging even if time is short, as the cumulative effect of the repeated ‚Äòit was good‚Äô and the climactic ‚Äòvery good‚Äô carries its own weight.
chapters.pastorals3:
Some in the group may be anxious about how to relate the Genesis account to modern science. It is fine to acknowledge that Christians hold a range of views on this, and that the study is not taking a position on the mechanism or timescale of creation ‚Äî only on the theological claims the text makes. Focus on those.

The reflection question ‚Äî ‚ÄòWhat does it mean to you to know you are made in God‚Äôs image?‚Äô ‚Äî can be surprisingly moving for people who have rarely been told that their life has inherent worth and dignity.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for the assumption that creation‚Äôs goodness is a naive claim in the face of suffering and brokenness. The chapter doesn‚Äôt ignore that ‚Äî it‚Äôs precisely the contrast between what God made and what sin has done to it that the next chapter explores.

chapters.keyPoints3:
This chapter is the hinge of the study: moving from the goodness of creation to the reality of what has gone wrong. It covers the origin of sin (Genesis 2‚Äì3), its nature (rebellion, not just bad behaviour ‚Äî Mark 7:14‚Äì23), its universality (Romans 3:9‚Äì12, 23), and its consequences (Romans 6:23).

The Common Misunderstanding callout on ‚Äòsin‚Äô is one of the most important in the study. The everyday reduction of sin to a list of very bad actions is exactly what prevents people from taking seriously that they themselves are sinners. The Biblical definition ‚Äî self at the centre rather than God ‚Äî needs time to land.

The closing bridge paragraph is deliberately honest: the picture is bleak. Don‚Äôt soften it. The good news of the following chapters only has force if this chapter has done its work.
chapters.pastorals3:
This is often the chapter where people most want to push back. Expect it. The claim that everyone has sinned without exception will feel unfair or extreme to some. Take Romans 3:9‚Äì12 seriously and let the text speak ‚Äî Paul anticipates the objection and addresses it directly.

Romans 7:21‚Äì25 ‚Äî Paul‚Äôs description of his own internal conflict ‚Äî is often personally resonant. Give people space to reflect on whether they recognise it in their own experience.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who hear the chapter as an attack on their character rather than a diagnosis of the human condition. The point is not that they are especially bad, but that all of us face the same problem.

chapters.keyPoints3:
This is where the good news begins. The chapter establishes that God‚Äôs response to sin is not condemnation but rescue ‚Äî and that rescue came at the highest possible cost. The key theological concept is substitutionary atonement: Jesus taking the penalty for our sin in our place (2 Corinthians 5:21; Galatians 3:13; 1 Peter 2:24).

Matthew 27:46 ‚Äî the cry of dereliction ‚Äî deserves careful attention. It is not a cry of despair but a theological event: the Son bearing the Father‚Äôs judgement on sin. The bridge paragraph after the atonement verses makes this explicit.

The Common Misunderstanding callout on ‚Äòsalvation‚Äô reframes the word as rescue ‚Äî which is its plain meaning and often much more accessible than the religious register in which it is usually heard.
chapters.pastorals3:
The section on those who do not believe (John 3:18; 2 Thessalonians 1:5‚Äì10) will likely be the most difficult for the group emotionally. Don‚Äôt skip it or rush it. The question ‚Äî ‚ÄòHow does this sit with you?‚Äô ‚Äî is intentionally open-ended. Let people be honest.

Some may find the idea of substitutionary atonement morally troubling (‚ÄòHow can it be just for one person to take another‚Äôs punishment?‚Äô). This is worth addressing: the answer lies in the fact that it is God Himself, in the person of His Son, who bears the penalty ‚Äî not an arbitrary third party.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who hear ‚ÄòJesus died for us‚Äô as a familiar phrase they have never really stopped to think about. This chapter is designed to make them stop and think about exactly what that means.

chapters.keyPoints3:
The resurrection is not an optional extra to the Christian faith ‚Äî Paul makes this explicit in 1 Corinthians 15:14‚Äì19. This chapter covers the evidence for the resurrection (empty tomb, post-resurrection appearances, over 500 witnesses), its significance (death defeated, future resurrection guaranteed), and its continuing implications (Jesus reigning, interceding, and returning).

The bridge paragraph on Jesus‚Äô present ministry (‚ÄòHe is our great High Priest, and He never stops‚Äô) addresses a common assumption: that Jesus finished his work at the cross and ascension. Hebrews 7:25 corrects this ‚Äî His intercession is continuous and present.

John 14:2‚Äì3 closes the chapter on the forward-looking promise of Christ‚Äôs return and the believer‚Äôs eternal home with Him. Don‚Äôt lose this in the apologetic material earlier in the chapter.
chapters.pastorals3:
The resurrection is the point at which many people feel the Christian claim becomes hardest to accept. Acknowledge this honestly. The chapter does not try to prove the resurrection philosophically but to present the historical evidence the New Testament offers and ask what best explains it.

The reflection question ‚Äî ‚ÄòWhich verse gives you the greatest comfort or strength?‚Äô ‚Äî is a good way to close. It moves from the apologetic register of the chapter into something more personal and devotional.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who treat the resurrection as a metaphor (Jesus ‚Äòlives on‚Äô in memory or influence). The passages in this chapter ‚Äî particularly Luke 24:36‚Äì43 and 1 Corinthians 15:5‚Äì8 ‚Äî are insisting on a physical, bodily, historical event.

chapters.keyPoints3:
This final chapter asks the question the whole study has been building toward: how do we respond? The two responses the Bible calls for are repentance and faith ‚Äî and the chapter is careful to hold them together as two sides of the same turning, not two separate steps.

The Common Misunderstanding callout on ‚Äògrace‚Äô is crucial here. Salvation is entirely God‚Äôs gift (Ephesians 2:8‚Äì9) ‚Äî not a reward for effort, goodness, or religious activity. This must be clear before the call to repentance is heard correctly, or repentance itself becomes a work we perform to earn God‚Äôs favour.

The closing section on changed living (Romans 6:15, 18) makes clear that grace is not a licence to continue in sin. Those who have been freed from sin have become servants of righteousness. The Christian life is not a static event but an ongoing reorientation.
chapters.pastorals3:
This is the chapter most likely to prompt a personal response. Be alert to people who are ready to make a commitment but don‚Äôt know how to do it, or who feel they have too much to give up. Be ready to talk personally with individuals after the group session.

The distinction between repentance as genuine inward change (not just regret or reform) and faith as active trust (not just intellectual assent) is important and worth drawing out carefully. Both can be counterfeited, and both can be misunderstood.
chapters.watch3: üëÅÔ∏è Watch for people who hear the call to repentance and conclude that they must clean up their life before they can come to God. The order in the gospel is the opposite: come as you are, and God will begin the transformation from there.

paras: Rick Creighton grew up in Northern Ireland and studied maths at Oxford and then taught maths at a secondary school in England. After that, he studied theology at Moore Theological College in Sydney and London School of Theology.

He served as a church pastor in England for ten years, before moving to Nigeria in 2012, where he has been teaching at ECWA Theological Seminary Kagoro and Jos ECWA Theological Seminary. He has been involved with BeaconLight Training since 2003.
Rick is married to Alanna. They have three children: Asha, Conor, and Jack.

publisher.name: BeaconLight
paras: BeaconLight is a non-denominational Christian training organisation based in the UK, founded in 1995. Its aim is to equip Christians worldwide with practically-applied Biblical gospel ministry tools ‚Äî resources designed to help people both equip themselves and equip others.

BeaconLight‚Äôs resources include CrossCheck, which enables Christians to explain their faith and lead others to Jesus, and Word@Work, a verse-by-verse applied devotional commentary of the whole New Testament. BeaconLight also provides printed Biblical resources to prisons, seafarers, police, and the armed services.
BeaconLight believes that Christian ministry should be free of charge, and all its online materials are available without registration or payment. The ministry is funded entirely by donations.
BeaconLight is a member of the Evangelical Alliance and Global Connections, and is a registered charity in England and Wales (Charity No. 1047046).
